title: Business Development Advisor - REMOTE - Fractional CFO Firm | Jacksonville / St. Johns County /
description:
**Business Development Advisor —** Fractional CFO Firm CFO Fuel LLC \| Jacksonville / St. Johns County / Green Cove Springs, FL \| 1099 Independent Contractor

**Job Summary**  
CFO Fuel is a fractional CFO firm serving trade contractors in construction, HVAC, plumbing, roofing, and electrical companies doing $2M–$7M in annual revenue across Northeast Florida. We help contractor business owners get clear on their numbers, protect their cash flow, and build businesses worth owning.

We are hiring one Business Development Advisor to identify, develop, and close new client engagements end\-to\-end. This is a professional B2B sales role in the financial services industry. You will build relationships with contractor business owners, run consultative discovery conversations, and close monthly retainer agreements. No cold pitching, no product demos, this is advisory selling to business owners who have real financial problems we solve every day.

This role has a direct path to Sales Manager as CFO Fuel grows its advisor team.

**Duties**

* Identify and pursue new business opportunities through outreach via phone, email, and LinkedIn to trade contractor business owners
* Develop and manage a personal sales pipeline aligned with CFO Fuel's ideal client profile
* Lead consultative discovery conversations with prospective clients, identifying financial pain points and presenting solutions, you do not pitch
* Manage all prospect interactions and pipeline activity in HubSpot CRM
* Qualify all prospects against the Company's defined Ideal Client Profile before advancing to close
* Collaborate directly with the Director of Sales on complex deals and strategic outreach

**What CFO Fuel Provides**

* A qualified lead list and outreach tools already built and ready to use
* Established scripts, objection handling, and discovery frameworks built specifically for the trade contractor market
* Full HubSpot CRM access, a dialer for outbound calling, and an active Apollo outreach sequence
* Marketing materials and a lead magnet report to use in prospect conversations
* Ongoing lead generation support and direct collaboration with leadership

**What We Expect You to Bring:**

* The independence to own your pipeline and your results without being managed daily. We are a niche solution, so leads must be valued.
* The structure to build and work a consistent outreach rhythm every week
* The professionalism to represent CFO Fuel in every conversation with a contractor business owner

**Who We Are Looking For**

* Prior B2B sales experience selling a professional service to business owners — commercial insurance, business banking, equipment financing, staffing, or similar
* Comfortable with outbound phone outreach and consultative discovery conversations
* Familiar with HubSpot or similar CRM
* Local to Jacksonville, St. Johns, St. Augustine, Ponte Vedra, Orange Park, or Green Cove Springs strongly preferred
* Self\-directed where you manage your own time and are measured entirely on results

**Compensation**

This is a 100% commission\-based 1099 independent contractor role. No base salary.

Compensation is structured around a per\-client closing fee paid upon signed agreement and first collected payment, plus a monthly recurring commission on every active client you bring in — paid every month they remain with us.

Realistic annual earnings based on performance:

* Standard Performer: $60,000\-$70,000
* Consistent performer: $75,000–$90,000
* Strong performer: $100,000–$120,000\+

Full commission structure, accelerators, and performance bonuses are outlined during the interview process. This role has defined production expectations that are discussed during the interview process. It is designed for someone who is confident in their ability to perform.

Join CFO Fuel as a Business Development Advisor and help us expand our reach across Northeast Florida's trade contractor community. Your consultative mindset, relationship\-building skills, and drive for results will directly shape our growth. We are looking for someone who wants to build something, grow with us, and be rewarded for it long\-term

Pay: $60,000\.00 \- $120,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Professional development assistance

Work Location: Remote

title: REEP Corp- Fractional Vice President of Finance
description:
Fractional Vice President of Finance  


REEP — San Antonio, TX (Remote: Austin or Houston)  


REEP is seeking a Fractional VP of Finance to provide senior financial oversight and strategic guidance for a growing real estate investment platform.  


REEP is a technology\-driven real estate investment firm focused on acquiring and operating multifamily communities throughout Texas. Our platform manages 4,500\+ units and over $650M in assets under management. We actively leverage technology and artificial intelligence to improve operational efficiency, financial insight, and decision\-making across the organization.  


This role is ideal for a Controller, VP Finance, or CFO\-level professional seeking a flexible, high\-impact role with a respected investment firm.  


Estimated commitment: 10–20 hours per week.  


Compensation:  
Very competitive hourly compensation based on experience and qualifications.  


Key Responsibilities  


Oversee treasury and banking operations, including monitoring cash balances and managing company bank accounts  
Authorize and execute wire transfers and maintain strong liquidity management  
Oversee the accounts payable process to ensure accurate and timely vendor payments  
Provide general ledger oversight and accounting troubleshooting to support efficient month\-end close  
Review and approve monthly financial statements  
Prepare bi\-monthly cash flow forecasts to monitor liquidity and anticipate funding needs  
Support RAMP accounting entries and troubleshooting  
Provide strategic financial guidance on capital allocation and financial health  
Assist leadership with special financial projects, financial systems improvements, and audit preparation  
Contribute to a technology\-forward finance function, leveraging automation and AI tools to improve financial reporting, forecasting, and operational insight  


Qualifications  


10\+ years of experience in finance, accounting, or corporate treasury  
Experience as a Controller, VP Finance, CFO, or fractional finance leader  
Strong financial reporting, treasury, and cash flow forecasting expertise  
Experience in real estate investment, private equity, or real estate operations preferred  
CPA or strong accounting background preferred  
Interest in leveraging technology and AI tools to improve financial operations  


Location  


San Antonio headquarters with remote candidates considered in Austin or Houston, with periodic travel to San Antonio.  


Application Guidelines  


Only candidates currently residing in San Antonio, Austin, or Houston will be considered for this role.  


No recruiters, staffing agencies, or third\-party submissions will be accepted. Direct applicants only.

title: Controller
description:
**IronClad Consulting Services**, a fractional accounting and financial advisory service provider, is looking for a **Controller** to add to the team. Founded with a passion for people, IronClad focuses on transparency and an exceptional work environment for our team. We specialize in CFO, Controller, and Accounting support for companies ranging from startups to venture backed across the US. IronClad works with all industries including but not limited to Consumer Goods, Food \& Beverage, Consumer Products, E\-commerce, Technology, Non\-Profit, Business Services, Medical Device, \& Healthcare. If you are a self\-starter, hands on multi\-tasker and possess the ability to work independently, come join us!

**Responsibilities:**

* Monthly reporting \& analysis of companies’ financial statements
* Improve Cost and inventory accounting processes in clients’ financial environment
* Manage monthly close activities to GAAP standards
* Maintain omnichannel sales environments, reporting, and systems

· Evaluate accounting and internal control systems and implement new systems and processes

· Mentor and train IronClad accounting team

· Lead internal and external accounting staff through accounting duties as needed

· Document processes and procedures for internal and client use

* Manage client cash flow and budgeting processes
* Prioritize activities and meet internal and client deadlines
* Effective communication with internal and external teams to meet 24\-hr SLA

**Education, Experience, and Skills:**

* Minimum 5 years of Controller experience
* Bachelor’s Degree in accounting (required)
* Experience with Manufacturing, Consumer Product Goods, or E\-Commerce required
* Advanced experience with QuickBooks Online and Excel required, NetSuite experience (preferred)
* Excellent communications skills \- written, verbal and interpersonal \- and an ability to tailor communication style to diverse audiences, including executives and operators
* Proven Inventory Experience, inventory software experience a plus
* Ability to learn and adapt to different systems and clients
* Detail oriented with high degree of accuracy
* Ability to interact effectively at all levels of organizations
* Self\-Starter and ability to work independently or part of a team

This is a remote position for full\-time employment. Employees provide their own work\-from\-home setup to include a laptop and Microsoft 365 software. We have an exceptional team and our highest priorities are our employees and clients!

*At IronClad Consulting Services we recognize, respect and value the contributions of each employee, and we strive to cultivate a work environment that strikes a balance between our professional and personal lives. Our core values guide us to seek out a broad range of perspectives, talent, and backgrounds to achieve our mission; one where all staff contribute to and share our success. We are committed to diversity, and as an equal opportunity employer, we are committed to employment practices that ensure that employees and applicants for employment are provided with equal opportunities without regard to race, color, national origin, ancestry, sex, age, religion, physical or mental disability, medical condition, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, genetic information or any other*

Job Type: Full\-time

Pay: $63\.00 \- $66\.00 per hour

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* Dental insurance
* Disability insurance
* Flexible schedule
* Health insurance
* Paid time off
* Referral program
* Vision insurance

Work Location: Remote

title: Financial Controller
description:
**Position Title:** Financial Controller  

**Location:** Remote

 **About the Firm**  

52TEN is a private investment firm based in Scottsdale, Arizona specializing in the acquisition and reposition of manufactured housing communities across the United States. As one of the top 100 community owners in the country, we are focused on delivering exceptional experiences for our residents, investors, partners, and team members.


Our current portfolio includes approximately 1,800 manufactured housing lots and $140M in assets under management, and we are continuing to grow through disciplined acquisitions and operational excellence.  

  

**About the Role**  

As we continue to scale our national portfolio, we are seeking a Financial Controller to lead the accounting function and ensure our financial infrastructure remains accurate, scalable, and investor\-grade.  

  

This role will oversee financial reporting, month\-end close, investor distributions, and the accounting team while supporting leadership with reliable financial insights. The Controller will play a key role in maintaining the integrity of our financial statements, improving accounting processes, and building systems that support continued portfolio growth.  

  

The ideal candidate is detail\-oriented, systems\-driven, and thrives in a fast\-paced environment where accuracy, accountability, and collaboration are essential.

 **Key Responsibilities:**  

  

Accounting \& Financial Reporting:

* Lead the month\-end and year\-end close processes in accordance with GAAP.
* Maintain accurate financial reporting across multiple property and fund entities.
* Oversee consolidation of property, fund, and corporate financial statements.
* Ensure balance sheet integrity through disciplined reconciliation and review processes.


Operations \& Portfolio Support:

* Partner with the operations team to provide financial insights that support data\-driven decision\-making and park\-level performance improvements.
* Collaborate with acquisitions and underwriting teams by providing historical operating insights and cost benchmarks to inform acquisition assumptions.


Cash \& Investor Reporting:

* Oversee quarterly investor distributions and financial reporting.
* Support cash flow reporting and forecasting alongside the Fractional CFO and leadership team.


Compliance \& Controls:

* Maintain strong internal controls and accounting processes to ensure financial accuracy and compliance.
* Coordinate with external tax advisors and auditors to support annual filings and investor K\-1 reporting.


Team Leadership:

* Lead and mentor the accounting team while fostering a high\-performance, collaborative culture.
* Build scalable processes, documentation, and workflows to support continued growth.

 **What Makes 52TEN Different?**

* **The Best Experience**  

We believe great companies start with great teams. We celebrate wins, support each other, and strive to create the best experience for everyone we work with.
* **True Remote Work**  

Enjoy the flexibility of a fully remote environment with autonomy to manage your schedule and do your best work from anywhere.
* **A Seat at the Table**  

This role is part of the leadership team and will have direct influence on company decisions and strategic direction.
* **Unlimited PTO (That You’re Encouraged to Take)**  

We believe balance matters. Our unlimited PTO policy is designed to help you recharge and bring your best self to work.
* **A World\-Class Team**  

We hire growth\-minded individuals who care deeply about collaboration, learning, and delivering exceptional results together.

 **Why Work With 52TEN?**


At 52TEN, culture is one of the most defining aspects of our success. We’re a fast\-paced, goal\-oriented company that runs on the Entrepreneurial Operating System (EOS), giving our team clear priorities, accountability, and the ability to execute at a high level.


At the same time, we believe work should complement your life—not consume it. As a fully remote team, we provide the flexibility, autonomy, and trust needed for our people to do meaningful work while maintaining balance.


While each team member brings unique expertise and responsibilities, collaboration is at the heart of everything we do. We rally around shared goals, support one another, and work together to deliver the best experience for our investors, partners, residents, and team.  

  

**Qualifications:**

* Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field
* 10\+ years of progressive accounting or financial management experience
* Real estate or property management accounting experience required
* Strong knowledge of GAAP and financial reporting standards
* Experience managing multi\-entity accounting environments
* Strong leadership, analytical, and communication skills
* Advanced Excel skills and experience with accounting software

 **Preferred Qualifications:**

* Certified Public Accountant (CPA) license strongly preferred
* Experience with property management accounting systems such as Rent Manager
* Experience in process improvement and accounting system optimization
* Familiarity with real estate investment structures and property\-level financials

 **Compensation and Benefits:**

* Salary Range: $140,000\-$180,000 depending on experience
* Performance\-based bonus opportunity
* Unlimited Paid Time Off
* Company paid holidays
* Group medical, dental, and vision, and life insurance
* Technology reimbursement
* Wellness reimbursement

 **Who We Are at 52TEN**

* A high\-performing team with the agility of a boutique company and the capability of a large one
* Forward\-thinking and tech\-savvy, always seeking smarter ways to work
* Deeply collaborative—both at our communities and across the company
* Committed to investing in our people, equipping you with the tools to thrive in your role and grow beyond it

**Who We’re Not**

* We’re not stuck in old ways of thinking or focused only on the bottom line
* We don’t forget that we’re people first—with families, ambitions, and values
* We don’t shy away from challenges or growth—we welcome them
* We don’t avoid accountability or repeat mistakes—we learn and evolve
* We don’t operate in silos—we win as one team

  

If this sounds like the right fit for you, we’d love to hear from you. Thanks for considering 52TEN as part of your career journey!

title: Partner, Finance (Colorado Residents Only)
description:
**About Ampleo**

**Cross\-Functional Leadership and Execution to Fuel Growth**

**We provide the expertise to accelerate your business at every stage and in every function.**

**Since 1996, Amplēo has helped thousands of businesses grow through on\-demand finance leadership, starting with fractional CFOs and outsourced controllers. What began as strategic financial support has evolved into a complete suite of cross\-functional services.**

**Today, Amplēo delivers embedded expertise in finance, HR, marketing, turnaround and restructuring, sales tax, and business valuation. Our professionals integrate directly into your team to provide both strategic insight and hands\-on execution, helping you achieve meaningful results faster.**

**We don’t just advise. We help you move, build, and win.**

 **Position Summary**


The Partner, Finance is a senior\-level finance professional responsible for driving business development, strengthening client relationships, and delivering high\-impact financial advisory services. This role serves as a strategic advisor to clients while contributing to revenue growth, cross\-functional collaboration, and the continued development of the company’s finance practice.

  

The Partner, Finance combines executive\-level financial expertise with business development leadership to expand market presence, support client success, and contribute to the growth of the organization.

  

This is a performance\-driven role for a finance executive who thrives in growth environments and is motivated by building and scaling a book of business.

 **Key Responsibilities**

* Identify, pursue, and close new client engagements within target industries and markets
* Build and manage a consistent pipeline of qualified prospects
* Develop referral networks and strategic partnerships
* Represent Amplēo at networking events, industry forums, and business development meetings
* Collaborate with marketing and internal teams to support lead generation initiatives
* Cross\-sell services across business units to increase total client value
* Maintain accountability for revenue targets and growth objectives
* Ensure financial integrity through oversight of financial reporting, budgeting, cash flow management, and risk mitigation while maintaining compliance with accounting standards and regulations.
* Serve as a trusted financial advisor to clients and internal stakeholders, translating financial data into actionable insights that improve profitability, operational performance, and strategic planning.
* Provide strategic financial guidance to leadership and clients by developing financial strategies, forecasting growth scenarios, and advising on key business decisions that drive long\-term value.
* Assess client needs and recommend appropriate fractional CFO or finance services
* Strengthen client retention through proactive engagement and measurable results
* Identify account expansion opportunities and renewal strategies
* Provide strategic oversight and mentorship to finance consultants as needed
* Contribute to the development of finance service offerings and go\-to\-market strategy
* Support the finance practice in scaling capabilities and market positioning

### **Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Business, or related field (or equivalent professional experience).
* 8\+ years of progressive experience in FP\&A, corporate finance, consulting, or executive financial leadership roles.
* Experience in cybersecurity, Quantum, robotics, deep\-tech, and similar
* Demonstrated success in business development and closing professional services engagements
* Strong executive presence and consultative selling ability
* Ability to build relationships with C\-suite leaders and business owners
* Experience owning or growing a book of business preferred
* Excellent communication, negotiation, and presentation skills
* Strong financial acumen and ability to assess business performance quickly
* Preferred:  

	+ CPA, CMA, MBA, or similar professional credential
	+ Prior CFO, fractional CFO, or consulting experience
	+ Experience in professional services, advisory, or consulting environments
* Applicants must be currently located in Colorado and authorized to work in the United States.

**Benefits**

* Flexible PTO
* Medical, dental, and vision insurance with FSA options
* Company\-paid life insurance
* Paid parental leave
* 401(k) with company match
* Professional development opportunities
* 12 paid holidays off

title: Corporate Controller
description:
YOU

  

You do your best work when the work is done right.

  

Not roughly right. Not good enough for now. Right — reconciled, documented, reviewed, and defensible. You take quiet satisfaction in a clean close, a balanced sheet, and a process that runs the same way every time because you built it that way.

  

You don't need the spotlight. You need accurate data, clear ownership, and a team that holds itself to your standard. When something is off, you find it. When a process is broken, you fix it — permanently, not temporarily.

  

You communicate carefully. You think before you speak. You'd rather be thorough than fast, and you've learned that those two things don't have to be in conflict if the systems are right.

  

If this sounds like how you're wired, keep reading.

  

THE ROLE

  

We are a growing property management company looking for a Corporate Controller to own the integrity of our financial operations. This is a senior role with real authority and real accountability — you'll lead the Accounting team, own the month\-end close, and serve as the financial backbone of a company that moves fast and expects precision.

  

You'll report directly to the COO and work alongside a fractional CFO, which means your voice carries weight and your work is visible. There's no bureaucracy to hide behind — the financials are yours.

  

WHAT YOU'LL OWN

* The close. Month\-end, year\-end, accruals, reconciliations — done accurately and on schedule, every time.

* The team. You'll lead and develop the Accounting team, set the standard, and build capacity that scales with the company.

* Internal controls. Design them, document them, test them, and fix them when they fail. You are the last line of defense on financial integrity.

* The balance sheet. AR, AP, cash, fixed assets — reconciled, current, and explainable at any moment.

* GAAP compliance and audit readiness. You know the rules and you follow them. Audit findings are resolved, not buried.

* Data you can trust. Financial and operational data that is accurate, consistently defined, and ready to drive decisions — not just reports.

* Investor reporting support. Property\-level financials that are clean, reconciled, and distribution\-ready before every reporting cycle.

  

WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

  

 You are a technical expert in accounting. GAAP is not a reference document — it's second nature.

  

 You build systems, not workarounds. When a process breaks, your instinct is to fix the root cause, document the fix, and train the team.

  

 You are precise under pressure. The close doesn't slip because you're busy. It's too important.

  

 You lead by standard. Your team knows what right looks like because you show them every day.

  

 You are calm, methodical, and thorough. You don't chase trends. You execute fundamentals at a high level, consistently.

  

 You own your outcomes. When something is wrong, you say so and you fix it. No deflection, no excuses.

  

QUALIFICATIONS

* CPA required (active)
* 5\+ years in accounting with at least 2 years in a Controller or senior accounting leadership role
* Demonstrated experience with month\-end close, balance sheet reconciliation, and internal controls
* Experience with property management or real estate accounting a strong plus
* AppFolio or comparable property management software experience preferred
* Proven ability to lead and develop accounting staff
* Experience supporting audit processes (internal or external)

  

WHO WE ARE

  

We are a values\-driven property management company built on Extreme Ownership, operational discipline, and a genuine commitment to the people and properties we serve. We don't talk about our culture — we work in it every day.

  

We are growing, and we are intentional about how we grow. That means we hire people who care about doing the work right, who hold themselves accountable before anyone has to ask, and who understand that a great company is built one good decision at a time.

  

If you want a role where your precision matters, your judgment is trusted, and the bar is high — this is it.

  

TO APPLY

  

Submit your resume directly.

  

We review every application carefully. We'll be in touch if there's a fit.

 *Equal Opportunity Employer*

*Real Property Management Express is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to building an inclusive workplace where all employees are treated with respect and provided equal opportunity regardless of background or protected status.*

title: Fractional CFO
description:
**Job Overview**

WACCPA is a growing CPA firm looking for a **Service Delivery Lead** to own the execution of our Fractional CFO department.

Currently, our CFO services are led by our Principal CPA. We are looking for a right\-hand professional to take over the day\-to\-day financial leadership for our clients, build out scalable reporting processes, and eventually transition into a full\-time Leadership role as our portfolio expands.

**Note:** This position begins as a **Part\-Time or Project\-Based Contract (1099\)** with a direct, transparent path to a **Full\-Time Lead** role as we hit specific growth milestones.

**What You’ll Do**

* **Client Financial Leadership:** Serve as the primary "Controller" for a portfolio of small\-to\-mid\-sized businesses.
* **The "Month\-End" Engine:** Oversee the closing process to ensure all client data is 100% accurate before it reaches the CFO/Principal level.
* **Advisory \& Insights:** Move beyond the spreadsheets. You will prepare monthly "Insight Packages" (using tools like Fathom, Reach, or Jirav) that explain the *why* behind the numbers.
* **Process Optimization:** Help us build the "standard operating procedures" (SOPs) for how our firm delivers CFO\-level value.
* **Creativity:** Prepare "What if" scenarios based on the direction of the client's short term and long term goals.

**Who You Are**

* **Experience:** 10\+ years of accounting experience, with at least 2 years in a Controller or Finance Director capacity.
* **Tech:** You have expertise and certification with QuickBooks Online, Xero, and modern reporting/forecasting apps.
* **Communication:** You can explain a Balance Sheet to a creative CEO who hates math.
* **Growth:** You aren't just looking for a task list; you want to help build a department.

**Compensation \& Growth Path**

* **Initial Phase:** $75 – $100 per hour (commensurate with experience).
* **Hours:** Starting at 10–20 hours per month with our current client base.
* **The Vision:** Our goal is to transition this role into a **Full\-Time Salaried Position ($120k \- $150k \+ Bonuses)** as the department hits revenue targets.

**Requirements**

* Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting or Finance (CPA or CMA preferred).
* Proven experience managing multiple client entities simultaneously.
* Recent experience in a fractional CFO role
* Must be based in PDT time zone and available for remote video meetings during standard business hours.

Pay: $75\.00 \- $100\.00 per hour

Work Location: Remote

title: Fractional CFO
description:
**About Evolv CFO** 

Evolv CFO is a dynamic fractional CFO firm helping growth\-stage businesses unlock financial clarity and strategic direction. We specialize in transforming messy books into clean, actionable insights and guiding companies through rapid scaling, fundraising, and operational optimization. Our clients range from solar and roofing firms to tech startups and service businesses, typically generating $2M–$15M in annual revenue.

**Role Overview**

**The Role**

At **EvolvCFO**, we provide high\-level financial leadership that isn’t afraid to get into the weeds. We are seeking a **Fractional CFO** who thrives on variety and possesses "full\-stack" accounting mastery. This isn't just a high\-level advisory role; you must be as comfortable managing ground\-floor bookkeeping, payroll, and reconciliations as you are delivering monthly reporting and sophisticated financial analysis.

We need a "financial detective"—someone who can dive headfirst into a complex problem, pull the necessary strings, and perform the research required to figure out exactly what is going on, even when the answer isn't obvious.

**Responsibilities**

* Lead financial planning, budgeting, and forecasting processes
* Implement and manage monthly close and reporting cadence
* Oversee cash flow management and strategic financial modeling
* Support fundraising efforts (debt/equity)
* Transition clients from cash to accrual accounting where applicable
* Develop KPI dashboards and performance tracking systems
* Collaborate with internal bookkeepers and client teams
* Provide strategic insights and leadership support to CEOs and boards

**Qualifications**

* CPA or equivalent financial certification preferred
* 7\+ years of experience in financial leadership roles
* Proven success in scaling businesses or managing financial turnarounds
* Strong command of QuickBooks Online, Harvest, and other financial tools
* Excellent communication and decision\-making skills
* Experience with startups, service businesses, or high\-growth environments

**Engagement Structure**

* Month\-to\-month contract with performance\-based incentives
* Weekly client meetings and monthly strategic reviews
* Remote work with optional in\-person strategy sessions

**Why Join Evolv CFO?**

* Work with passionate founders and fast\-growing companies
* Make a tangible impact on business growth and financial health
* Be part of a collaborative, entrepreneurial team
* Enjoy autonomy, flexibility, and meaningful client relationships

Job Type: Full\-time

Pay: $90,000\.00 \- $110,000\.00 per year

Work Location: Remote

title: Fractional CFO at Kelly Strayhorn Theater
description:
**ARE YOU OUR NEXT FRACTIONAL CFO?**


Kelly Strayhorn Theater seeks a **Fractional Chief Financial Officer (CFO)** to provide senior\-level financial leadership during a period of organizational growth, capital planning and strategic transition.


Working closely with Co\-Executive Directors, Finance Committee, and Board of Directors, the Fractional CFO will strengthen financial oversight, improve forecasting and cash management, and support long\-term financial sustainability.


This role serves as a **strategic advisor and senior financial reviewer**, providing final oversight, analysis, and guidance across financial systems, while partnering with in\-house staff and third\-party accounting support. This role does not perform day\-to\-day bookkeeping or payroll processing, but ensures accuracy, clarity, and alignment across all financial functions.

 **ABOUT KELLY STRAYHORN THEATER**


Named after 20th\-century entertainment legends and Pittsburgh natives Gene Kelly and Billy Strayhorn, Kelly Strayhorn Theater is a home for creative experimentation, community dialogue, and collective action rooted in the liberation of Black and queer people. KST takes a multidisciplinary approach to presenting contemporary performing arts while examining the questions that define and inspire us as individuals and communities.

  


**ENGAGEMENT STRUCTURE**
This is a **fractional / part\-time contract (1099\) engagement.** This role is not eligible for benefits.  

While some work may be conducted remotely, periodic in person engagement will support the effectiveness of this role.


This role is expected to include:

* **Approximately 12 hours per month** for ongoing operational and financial oversight  

with additional time during audit and budget preparation, which are considered part of the core operational scope
* **Approximately 8\+ hours per month** dedicated to capital campaign support during capital campaign


Time commitment will fluctuate based on organizational needs and is expected to increase during:

* Annual budget development (with Board approval typically at the June Board meeting)
* Audit preparation and review periods
* Periods of heightened capital campaign activity
* Active financing efforts or lender engagement


KST’s fiscal year runs September 1 – August 31\. Time commitment is expected to scale as the capital campaign advances and as financing structures become more active.

 **COMPENSATION**


The Fractional CFO will receive a $2,000 monthly stipend for ongoing operational and financial oversight as described in this agreement.


Additional work related to the capital campaign or other special projects will be compensated at an additional hourly rate or project\-based stipend, to be mutually agreed upon in advance and aligned with the scope and budget of the project.

  


**SCOPE OF WORK**
The Fractional CFO will provide strategic financial leadership and oversight in the following areas:

**FINANCIAL OVERSIGHT, CONTROLS \& SYSTEMS**

* Provide senior\-level review and oversight of the General Ledger, ensuring accuracy, consistency, and appropriate classification in alignment with nonprofit, funder, and audit standards.
* Serve as a trusted resource for accounting questions, financial controls, and complex financial decision\-making.
* Oversee and advise on financial policies, internal controls, and risk management practices as organizational needs evolve.
* Work in partnership with in\-house bookkeeping staff and a third\-party accounting firm that performs month\-end close, providing final review and approval of financial statements.

### **CASH MANAGEMENT, INVESTMENTS \& LIQUIDITY**

* Advise on cash management strategy, including operating reserves, liquidity planning, and timing of cash inflows and outflows.
* Provide guidance on investment strategies appropriate for a nonprofit organization, including CDs, CD ladders, reserve accounts, and risk\-appropriate vehicles.
* Support leadership in aligning cash availability, earned revenue cycles, and restricted funds, with strict oversight of restricted funding requirements to safeguard financial stability.

### **REPORTING, GOVERNANCE \& EXTERNAL COMMUNICATION**

* Prepare and/or review monthly and periodic financial reports for the Finance Committee and Board, including narrative analysis and interpretation
* Attend bi\-monthly Board and Committee meetings as required, particularly for audit review and annual budget discussions.
* Support reporting to banks, funders, and external stakeholders, ensuring clarity, accuracy, and alignment with covenants, grant requirements, and capital commitments.
* Assist leadership in responding to complex financial inquiries from funders, lenders, and board members.

### **BUDGETING, FORECASTING \& FINANCIAL PLANNING**

* Advise on and review annual and multi\-year operating budgets developed by staff, ensuring alignment with strategic goals and financial sustainability.
* Develop and maintain multi\-year financial forecasts and scenario models to support planning and decision\-making.
* Lead cash\-flow forecasting and expense projection to support proactive management and early identification of risks or opportunities.

### **CAPITAL CAMPAIGN, FINANCING \& STRATEGIC INITIATIVES**

* Support the creation, review, and ongoing updating of the capital project budget, including cash flows, cost assumptions, timing, funding sources, and related campaign reporting.
* Serve as a financial strategy partner for the capital campaign, with increasing engagement as the campaign becomes more active, including preparation of campaign financial reports.
* Develop financial modeling related to campaign timing, cash flow, debt capacity, and project phasing to inform capital and financing decisions.
* Assist in evaluating, securing, and managing financing, including preparation for lender conversations, analysis of financing options, and review of terms and implications.
* Contribute financial insight to business planning and major organizational initiatives.

### **AUDIT \& COMPLIANCE SUPPORT**

* Prepare financial statements for review by auditors (including footnotes), as well as supporting schedules and information needed for 990\.
* Provide final oversight of audit materials, schedules, and responses, ensuring accuracy and readiness.
* Support leadership in addressing audit findings, recommendations, and follow\-up actions.

 **DESIRED EXPERIENCE**

* Senior\-level financial leadership experience, including work as a CFO, fractional CFO, or equivalent role.
* Demonstrated experience with nonprofit financial management, including audits, board reporting, funder compliance, and restricted funds.
* Strong expertise in cash\-flow management, forecasting, financial modeling, and capital planning.
* Experience advising organizations through capital campaigns, financing strategies, and real estate or facility transitions, including working with boards, lenders, and campaign leadership.
* Ability to translate complex financial information into clear, actionable insights for non\-financial stakeholders.
* Experience working within the Pittsburgh and regional philanthropic and financial landscape is valuable, especially given the organization’s engagement with local foundations, lenders, and public funding partners.

 **ESSENTIAL QUALITIES**

* Commitment to diversity, equity, inclusion, accessibility, and belonging and the willingness to prioritize and foster a commitment to valuing differences of race, ethnicity, age, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, religion, ability, and socio\-economic circumstances.
* Strategic, steady partner comfortable working with executive leadership and boards.
* Systems\-oriented thinker who balances detail with long\-term vision.  

Independent, proactive contractor who brings clarity, rigor, and calm to complex financial decisions.
* High level of discretion and integrity when handling sensitive financial information.

 **TIMELINE**  

Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis, with **initial review beginning March 25, 2026**. Interviews will begin shortly thereafter and continue until the role is filled. We anticipate the engagement beginning in **early May 2026**, following completion of a background check.  

Applicants are encouraged to apply by **March 25** for priority consideration.

 **HOW TO APPLY**


We welcome applicants from all backgrounds who are energized by KST’s mission and values. Research supports that people whose voices have historically been underrepresented in leadership spaces are often less likely to apply unless they meet every requirement. If this role interests you but you do not meet every listed qualification, we still encourage you to apply.  

  

Applications are only accepted through our **Jazz HR portal, here****.**

title: Client Advisory Services Manager
description:
Councilor, BuchananMitchell, P.C. (CBM), a leading accounting and business advisory firm, is seeking a Manager\-level professional who operates as a fractional CFO for a portfolio of outsourced accounting and advisory clients, with a focus on nonprofit organizations and professional service businesses. This role combines technical accounting oversight with forward\-looking financial leadership. The Manager owns client relationships, leads engagement delivery, and translates financial information into insights for client leadership.


The ideal candidate will bring **at least six years** of public accounting, advisory and/or finance leadership experience, including experience operating at a manager or engagement\-lead level with responsibility for supervising staff and owning client deliverables. Experience in a Client Advisory Services (CAS) or outsourced accounting environment is strongly preferred, as this role requires managing multiple client engagements simultaneously. This role is ideal for a detailoriented, analytical professional who excels in a dynamic, clientfocused environment. This position includes the opportunity to help strengthen delivery today while expanding the firm's advisory capabilities and is designed for a professional who wants to grow into a future leadership role within the practice.

*Remote work and flexible schedule are available.*

 **Essential Functions**

*Client Advisory and Forward\-Looking Financial Leadership*

* Conduct comprehensive financial analyses to identify trends, assess performance, and develop datadriven insights for client decisionmaking
* Develop, maintain, and interpret financial models supporting cash flow forecasting, revenue and expense projections, and key performance indicators
* Translate budgettoactual results into actionable recommendations to improve financial and operational outcomes
* Assist clients in preparing annual budgets, multiyear financial plans, and rolling forecasts
* Analyze profitability by program, department, or business unit and identify opportunities for financial efficiency
* Produce dashboards and financial analytics reports for internal and external stakeholders
* Assess financial implications of business scenarios, new initiatives, and organizational changes
* Collaborate with client leadership to interpret financial results, establish metrics, and strengthen financial stewardship
* Present financial information and analysis to executive leadership, Boards of Directors, and Finance Committees

*Engagement Ownership and Client Management*

* Own engagements from planning through delivery, including deadlines, staffing coordination and client communication
* Serve as the lead advisor on complex or non\-routine client matters, addressing them directly coordinating appropriate team members and resources to reach resolution
* Maintain accountability for delivery of agreed scope of services and identify opportunities for additional services that enhance value for the client
* Maintain proactive, consistent communication with clients and team members to support strong working relationships
* Coordinate with tax and other specialty teams to support integrated client service delivery

*Accounting Team Oversight and Development*

* Review financial statements and other key deliverables prepared by controller\- and senior\-level staff for accuracy, consistency, and advisory value
* Lead and mentor accounting staff assigned to engagements, providing direction, review, and professional development
* Oversee accounting operations staff, including those providing A/R, A/P, payroll, and general ledger functions, to support reliable financial reporting
* Foster proactive and consistent communication with staff, partners, and cross\-functional teams to maintain alignment on deadlines, priorities, and client needs
* Strengthen processes and workflows to improve efficiency, scalability, and service delivery
* Promote a culture of accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement within the team

**Other Functions**

* Demonstrate strong organizational, timemanagement and analytical skills
* Continue developing technical, industry and advisory expertise
* Support training and mentoring of personnel
* Build and strengthen client relationships through an entrepreneurial, servicefocused approach
* Participate in business development activities, including networking and referral development
* Perform other responsibilities consistent with the role

 **Job Qualifications**

* **Minimum of 6 years** of accounting and finance experience, preferably within a CAS or outsourced accounting environment in public accounting
* Experience operating at a manager level with responsibility for supervising staff and owning client deliverables
* Strong understanding of financial and internal controls within a GAAPcompliant environment
* CPA certification strongly preferred, or equivalent professional finance credential and experience
* Bachelor's degree in accounting or a related field required
* Master's degree in finance, or a related discipline, is preferred but not required
* Proficiency with accounting and financial software (QuickBooks Online, BILL, Sage Intacct, Xero, CCH Suite, etc.) or demonstrated ability to quickly learn new systems Advanced Excel and Microsoft Office skills, with ability to develop scalable forecasting and financial modeling tools
* Exposure to analytics or reporting platforms is a plus, particularly for candidates interested in helping evolve advisory capabilities
* Excellent analytical abilities with exceptional accuracy and attention to detail
* Ability to manage multiple client engagements, prioritize work, and meet deadlines
* Strong written and verbal communication skills
* Customerfocused team player who thrives in a dynamic environment

 **About CBM**


At CBM, it is our goal to hire talented, energetic people who are enthusiastic about what they do. We offer a mentorship program to encourage individuals to grow professionally and develop in their careers. CBM offers today's accounting professionals opportunities for advancement in a fast\-paced environment that allows for both professional success and work\-life balance.


CBM offers a diverse, flexible work environment, and we are proud to offer a competitive salary, a comprehensive benefits package, growth opportunities, and a team\-oriented environment.

**Company Benefits**

* 401(k) and Profit\-Sharing Plan
* 10 Paid Holidays
* Paid Time Off
* Bereavement/Jury Duty Leave
* Paid Parental Leave
* FSA/Dependent Care
* Life Insurance
* Short and Long\-Term Disability
* Volunteer Community Service Day
* Healthcare Benefits \- medical, dental, visionother programs
* Paid ParkingMetro
* Subsidized Employee Wellness and Fitness Program
* Mentorship Program \- Project Clear Path
* Professional TrainingDevelopment
* Tuition Reimbursement
* CPA and Other Certifications Assistance
* Professional Memberships
* Business Casual Work Environment
* Fun Firm Activities

 **\*\* CBM is an Equal Opportunity Employer dedicated to diversity and inclusion \*\***

title: Accounting & Tax Manager (CPA) — Director Track
description:
**About Orivon**

Orivon Advisory Group is a boutique advisory firm (approx. $1\.6M revenue) helping founders and middle\-market operators move from complexity to clarity—and from clarity to scale. We blend strategic finance, tax \& compliance, and CFO services to deliver measurable outcomes.

**The Opportunity**

We’re hiring a seasoned **Accounting \& Tax Manager (Active CPA)** who can lead client engagements end\-to\-end, **serve as a fractional CFO** when needed and grow into a **Director** role. You’ll own a portfolio, mentor a small team, and help build the systems that let a small firm punch above its weight.

**What You’ll Do**

**Client Leadership \& CFO Services**

* Act as lead advisor and fractional CFO for select clients (forecasting, cash flow, KPI dashboards, board/lender packs).
* Translate strategy into operating plans; run monthly/quarterly business reviews.

**Technical Accounting \& Tax**

* Oversee full\-cycle accounting and close (multi\-entity, multi\-state; GAAP compliance).
* Lead complex **tax preparation** (C\-corp, S\-corp, partnerships, trusts, HNW) and **tax resolution/controversy** (IRS/state notices, payment plans, penalty abatement, OIC).
* Review and sign off on workpapers, returns, and financial statements; maintain audit\-ready documentation.

**Practice Management**

* Manage and develop seniors/staff; establish QA standards and repeatable workflows.
* Champion automation and AI\-assisted tooling to reduce cycle times and errors.
* Scope engagements, price profitably, expand key accounts; partner with leadership on proposals/SOWs.

**Compliance \& Risk**

* Own compliance calendars; ensure on\-time filings and payments across jurisdictions.
* Monitor regulatory changes (IRS, state, FASB) and implement policy/process updates.

**What Makes You a Fit**

**Must\-Haves**

* **Active CPA** in good standing.
* **10\+ years** in a **CPA practice** with progressive responsibility.
* **5\+ years** as a **Manager** leading teams and client portfolios.
* Demonstrated fractional\-CFO capability for small/mid\-market clients.
* Deep experience in **tax prep** and **tax resolution** (controversy).
* Builder’s mindset: process design, documentation, and coaching.

**Nice\-to\-Haves**

* Multi\-entity/multi\-state/SALT exposure; industry depth in healthcare, tech/SaaS, professional services, logistics/e\-commerce, or nonprofits.
* Fluency with modern stacks (QBO Advanced/NetSuite/Xero; CCH/UltraTax/Drake; workflow/BI; OCR/AI tools).
* Comfort presenting to owners, boards, lenders, and regulators.

**What Success Looks Like (6–12 months)**

* 100% on\-time close and filing cadence across the portfolio.
* Client NPS ≥ 70; ≥ 90% retention; 2\+ account expansions or $150k\+ growth.
* Standardized workflows that cut cycle times 20–30%.
* Bench plan in place; documented playbooks for recurring work.
* Demonstrated leadership impact toward **Director** track.

**Compensation \& Benefits** 

* **Base Salary:** **$90,000 – $110,000** (Orlando, Atlanta, Jacksonville).
* **Bonus Target:** **12–20%** of base (up to 25% for CFO/turnaround impact) tied to portfolio margin, collections/DSO, on\-time delivery, and client NPS.
* Health, dental, vision; 401(k) with match; CPA license \& CPE support.
* Hybrid/flexible work; periodic on\-site client visits.

**Work Model**

Hybrid within **Orlando, FL; Atlanta, GA; or Jacksonville, FL**. Occasional travel for client meetings, team summits, or audits.

Job Type: Full\-time

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Jacksonville, FL 32216

title: Fractional Chief Financial Officer
description:
The Fractional CFO serves as a senior financial leader and strategic partner to the CEO. This role partners closely to strengthen financial performance, support data\-driven decision making, and guide the organization’s long\-term growth and sustainability.

This position is anticipated at 10 hours per week, with seasonality adjustments up to 20 hours per week. This position is remote but requires residency in Washington State due to operational and regulatory requirements.
  

**Responsibilities:**
**Strategic Financial Leadership**
* Serve as the CEO’s primary financial thought partner, translating financial data into clear, actionable insights.
* Advise on financial implications of strategic initiatives, growth opportunities, and operational changes.
* Develop and maintain multi\-year financial models and scenario analyses.

**Financial Planning and Analysis**
* Oversee budgeting, forecasting, and long\-range planning processes.
* Lead rolling forecasts and variance analysis with clear explanations and recommendations.
* Ensure financial information supports timely, informed executive decision\-making.

**Cash Flow, Risk, and Sustainability**
* Proactively manage and forecast cash flow, liquidity, and reserves.
* Identify financial, operational, and reimbursement risks early and recommend mitigation strategies.
* Advise on capital planning, major expenditures, and mitigating financial risk through contract analysis.

**Oversight \& Governance**
* Provide high\-level oversight of accounting functions performed by the Sr Accountant (without duplicating operational work).
* Ensure appropriate internal controls, financial policies, and reporting standards are in place.
* Support audits, external financial relationships, and stakeholders as needed.

**Reporting \& Communication**
* Prepare concise, CEO\- and board\-level financial dashboards and narratives.
* Present financial performance and outlook to leadership and board audiences as requested.
* Ensure financial information is understandable, accurate, and aligned with organizational priorities.


**Position descriptions represent a general outline of job duties, functions, and qualifications. They are not intended to be detailed or comprehensive in nature. Other duties or responsibilities may be assigned.**  

  

**Knowledge, Skills and Abilities**
* Must have advanced knowledge of financial management, accounting principles (GAAP), and internal controls.
* Must have advanced knowledge of strategic financial planning, budgeting, and forecasting methodologies.
* Must have advanced knowledge of cash flow management and working capital optimization.
* Must be a strategic thinker with the ability to translate financial data into actionable business insights
* Must possess executive\-level communication and presentation skills for board, shareholders and senior leadership.
* Must possess advanced knowledge of financial modeling, budget development, and variance analysis.
* Must have excellent interpersonal and customer services skills and must have the ability to establish and maintain effective relationships with all employees and vendors.
* Must be able to handle highly confidential information with discretion.
* Must be proficient in Microsoft Office applications, particularly Excel (including lookups and pivot tables), Outlook, Teams, Word and PowerPoint.
* Must have the ability to work well under pressure, handle multiple priorities and meet deadlines; must have the ability to work independently with minimal supervision.
* Must be able to establish and maintain professional, productive, and courteous interactions with PNW employees and providers.

**Education and Experience:**
Minimum Requirements
* Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Business Administration, Healthcare Administration or related field
* 8 \-10 years of progressive financial leadership experience including senior\-level responsibility

Preferred Requirements
* CPA, MBA, MHA or equivalent advanced credential
* Experience serving as a CFO or Fractional CFO within a healthcare organization
* Experience with provider compensation models and productivity metrics (e.g. RVUs)

title: Accounting Manager
description:
At EvolvCFO, our Account Managers are the glue that holds the client relationship together. If the CFO is the surgeon performing high\-level strategy, you are the Physician Assistant: managing the patient’s ongoing care, overseeing the technical "vitals" (the books), and ensuring every task is executed to perfection.  
You take the baton the moment the sales process ends. You aren't just a liaison; you are a technically\-sound accounting professional who ensures our "Financial Phases" are implemented and that the bookkeeping team stays on track.

**What You’ll Do**

* **Own the Onboarding:** Lead the kickoff process for new clients, setting the tone for a structured and professional partnership.
* **Technical Oversight:** Review full\-cycle accounting work (A/R, A/P, Reconciliations) to ensure 100% accuracy before it reaches the CFO or the client.
* **Bridge the Gap:** Act as the primary point of contact for clients on tactical and technical questions. You translate client needs into tasks for the bookkeeping team.
* **Project Management:** Keep the "train on the tracks." You ensure recurring monthly tasks and one\-off projects are completed on time, moving the client through Evolv’s financial growth phases.
* **Support the CFO:** Prepare and deliver the necessary financial reports and data sets so the CFO can focus on high\-level advisory work.

**Who You Are (Technical Requirements)**

* **The Accounting Expert:** You have a rock\-solid grasp of full\-cycle accounting. You’ve been in the trenches and know what "clean books" actually look like.
* **QBO Power User:** You are highly proficient in QuickBooks Online and familiar with the modern accounting tech stack (e.g., Bill.com, Expensify, Gusto).
* **Public Accounting/Firm Experience:** You understand the pace of managing multiple clients simultaneously. You don’t get overwhelmed by shifting priorities.
* **The "Manager" Mindset:** You are comfortable delegating to and reviewing the work of bookkeepers, providing feedback to ensure the EvolvCFO standard is met.

**Core Competencies**

* **Accuracy Obsessed:** You catch the double\-entry error or the miscategorized expense that others miss.
* **Relationship Driven:** You can explain a technical accounting issue to a non\-financial founder in a way that builds trust, not confusion.
* **Process Oriented:** You love a good checklist. You thrive on ensuring that "the way we do things" is the way things actually get done.

**Why EvolvCFO?**

* **The "Evolved" Way:** We don’t just "do books"—we change the trajectory of companies.
* **Autonomy:** You’ll have the space to manage your client portfolio as you see fit, backed by the support of a world\-class team.
* **Career Path:** This role is perfect for a Senior Accountant or Accounting Manager looking to move into the fractional executive space.

Pay: $55,000\.00 \- $65,000\.00 per year

Education:

* Bachelor's (Required)

Experience:

* Accounting: 3 years (Required)

Work Location: Remote

title: NOW HIRING - Chief Financial Officer - Financial Strategy Advisor
company: How To MANAGE A Small Law Firm
description:
HTM is hiring a **battle\-tested CFO** who has helped businesses become financially disciplined, profitable, and scalable—not just someone who produces financial reports.


This is **not a passive accounting or advisory role**. We are looking for a hands\-on financial operator who understands how to install financial systems, enforce accountability around numbers, and drive measurable improvements in profitability and cash flow.


To be considered, you must have **10\+ years of senior financial leadership experience** and a proven track record of improving financial performance in growing businesses.


You must also have **direct experience working with companies generating between $10M and $30M\+ in annual revenue**, where financial discipline, cash flow management, and operational accountability are critical to sustained growth.


If your background is limited to financial reporting without directly influencing profitability, pricing, cash flow, and operational decisions, this role will not be a fit.


**What This Role Really Is**
============================


You will serve as a **CFO\-level coach and financial execution partner** to law firm owners across the country.


This role blends **strategic financial leadership with disciplined execution and accountability**. You are not just reviewing numbers—you are helping owners understand them, act on them, and improve them.


Your role includes:


**Diagnosing Financial Issues**  

Identifying profitability leaks, pricing problems, expense inefficiencies, and cash flow risks.


**Driving Financial Outcomes**  

Turning financial insights into clear, actionable steps that improve margins, revenue discipline, and financial stability.


**Enforcing Financial Accountability**  

Holding firm owners accountable to financial targets, commitments, and operational discipline.


**A Day in the Life**
=====================


Conduct high\-level 1:1 financial strategy calls with law firm owners.


Review financial statements, cash flow reports, KPIs, and performance dashboards.


Help owners understand the **story behind their numbers** and what actions they need to take.


Identify risks and opportunities in pricing, expenses, staffing, and operational performance.


Guide owners through budgeting, forecasting, and forward\-looking financial decisions.


Document commitments and track measurable financial progress.


Respond to client communication within established white\-glove service standards.


Participate in internal leadership sessions focused on scaling the HTM mission.


**Required Experience (Non\-Negotiable)**
=========================================


**10\+ years of senior financial leadership experience** (CFO, Fractional CFO, or equivalent).


**Revenue Scale Experience:** Direct experience working with companies generating **$10M–$30M\+ in annual revenue**.


**Direct Financial Impact:** Proven record of improving profitability, cash flow, or financial performance in a business.


**Entrepreneurial Environment:** Experience working with small businesses, professional services firms, or founder\-led organizations.


**Financial Strategy:** Deep experience with financial forecasting, budgeting, and financial modeling.


**Financial Coaching:** Ability to teach non\-financial business owners how to make better financial decisions.


**Systems \& Metrics:** Experience building financial dashboards, KPIs, and performance tracking systems.


**Who This Role Is Perfect For**
================================


This role is ideal for a **financial operator who enjoys working directly with business owners and influencing real financial outcomes**—not just producing reports or managing internal accounting teams.


You will thrive in this role if you are:


**A Fractional or Advisory CFO**  

You have experience advising multiple businesses and enjoy helping entrepreneurs understand their numbers, improve profitability, and make better financial decisions.


**A Former CFO Who Wants More Direct Impact**  

You've worked in senior financial leadership and prefer being closer to the decision\-making process rather than operating inside a large corporate finance structure.


**A Financial Leader Who Enjoys Coaching Entrepreneurs**  

You like helping non\-financial founders understand their numbers and turn financial insights into practical business actions.


**An Operator Who Connects Numbers to Strategy**  

You naturally link financial performance to pricing, staffing, operational efficiency, and growth planning.


**Someone Who Values Measurable Outcomes**  

You enjoy seeing tangible results from your work—stronger margins, healthier cash flow, and more financially disciplined businesses.


**A Professional Who Thrives in Accountability\-Driven Environments**  

You are comfortable setting financial expectations, tracking progress, and holding business owners accountable to their commitments.


**Someone Who Wants to Influence Multiple Businesses**  

Instead of working inside one company, you enjoy helping many business owners improve their financial performance and decision\-making.


**This Role Is NOT for You If**
===============================


This position requires **direct financial leadership, candid conversations, and measurable results**. It may not be the right fit if you:


Prefer traditional accounting roles focused primarily on compliance and reporting.


Avoid challenging business owners when financial discipline is lacking.


Prefer highly structured corporate environments.


Dislike accountability around financial metrics and performance targets.


Prefer working behind the scenes rather than advising business leaders directly.


Have never influenced the financial outcomes of a growing business.

**Compensation**
================


* **Base Salary:** $110,000
* **Performance Pay:** Up to $182,000 annually depending on client roster  

Your earning potential scales with your impact — not your hours.


**Why Join HTM**
================


**National Impact**  

Help law firm owners build profitable, financially disciplined businesses.


**Executive\-Level Influence**  

Work directly with business owners on decisions that shape their companies.


**Remote Leadership Role**  

Operate as a senior financial leader in a flexible remote environment.


**Proven Framework**  

Leverage a system and curriculum already helping law firms scale successfully.


**High\-Accountability Culture**  

Work with a team that values **results, discipline, and measurable progress**.


If you are an execution\-focused leader who thrives on accountability and enjoys helping business owners follow through on what matters most, this role will challenge and reward you. **Apply only if you are ready to lead from the front and deliver real results.**


Please note this job description is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities that are required of the employee for this job. Duties, responsibilities and activities may change at any time with or without notice.


At this time, we are ***only*** able to hire new employees in the following states, **AZ, CO, FL, GA, IL, MD, MI, NC, NJ, NY, PA, TX, VA, WA.**


*HTM provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws.*

title: Chief Medical Officer
description:
**Job Title: Chief Medical Officer**

**About Infusion IV**

Infusion IV is a functional medicine focused infusion therapy and wellness clinic serving Colorado via a downtown Denver brick and mortar clinic and a metro area mobile delivery model. We offer IV therapy, injectables, NAD\+, peptides, hormone optimization, GLP\-1 weight loss protocols, iron infusions, ozone therapy, and longevity programming across clinic and mobile channels. Our mission is to deliver medically sound, standardized care at a level of quality and consistency that is rare in this space — and to build the infrastructure that makes that possible at scale.

We are in active growth, expanding our clinical footprint, service lines, and provider network. Functional medicine is not a feature of our brand — it is the foundation of it. The CMO will define what that foundation looks like for every patient we see and every provider we train from here forward.

**Position Overview**

The Chief Medical Officer is a senior executive role responsible for the clinical leadership, integrity, and direction of Infusion IV. The CMO carries full authority over clinical standards, treatment protocols, provider training, and regulatory compliance across all service lines — serving as the highest clinical voice in the company.

This is a fractional or part\-time engagement designed for an experienced clinician who is ready to practice their specialty at scale and build lasting clinical infrastructure, without taking on the operational weight of running a business. Infusion IV manages the business. The CMO leads the medicine.

**Key Responsibilities:** 

**Clinical Standards \& Oversight**

* Set and maintain clinical standards across all service lines and delivery channels
* Serve as the final escalation point for complex or high\-risk clinical situations
* Establish chart quality benchmarks and drive continuous improvement in outcomes and patient safety

**Protocol \& Systems Development**

* Build standardized, scalable treatment protocols and lab review frameworks that any qualified provider can execute consistently
* Establish documentation standards that meet regulatory requirements and reflect clinical best practices
* Design clinical onboarding for all incoming providers

**Provider Training \& Legacy**

* Train and credential incoming providers on protocols, standards, and Infusion IV's clinical philosophy
* Build the educational infrastructure that sustains care quality as we grow into new markets
* Serve as the clinical benchmark every future provider is measured against

**Compliance \& Regulatory Oversight**

* Ensure all clinical operations comply with Colorado scope\-of\-practice regulations and applicable state and federal standards
* Maintain proper oversight documentation for all delegated clinical services
* Stay current on regulatory developments affecting IV therapy, functional medicine, and telehealth in Colorado

**External Presence \& Influence**

* Represent Infusion IV clinically with referral partners, the medical community, and the public
* Participate in educational content and marketing materials as appropriate
* Support the development of provider referral relationships that strengthen our patient pipeline

**Requirements**

* Active, unrestricted Colorado medical license — MD, DO, ND, or NP
* Board certified or board eligible in a relevant specialty; valid DEA registration
* Minimum 3 years of clinical practice post\-licensure, with hands\-on experience in IV therapy, functional medicine, hormone optimization, or regenerative medicine
* Proficiency interpreting comprehensive lab panels and building structured, protocol\-driven care plans; experience with peptides, GLP\-1 agents, or TRT
* Operates with full autonomy in a fast\-moving environment; direct communicator who can set and hold a high clinical standard
* Comfortable serving as an internal clinical representative of a growing brand
* Proficient with EHR platforms and digital communication tools; responsive with a 48\-hour turnaround expectation on clinical inquiries
* Genuine passion for functional and longevity medicine as a field

**What We Offer**

* Fractional or part\-time engagement to start, with a flexible, self\-directed schedule
* Competitive retainer plus per\-consult compensation commensurate with executive clinical leadership
* Full operational support — Infusion IV manages the business and team so the CMO can lead the medicine
* Direct relationship with the founding team; no hierarchy, no gatekeepers
* Meaningful authority over clinical direction, provider standards, and service development
* Clear path to expanded scope and equity conversation as the company grows

**How to Apply**

title: Chief Medical Officer
description:
**American Senior Communities is now hiring a Chief Medical Officer**



Full\-time, Fractional, or Consulting arrangements will be considered



American Senior Communities is seeking an experienced, forward\-thinking **Chief Medical Officer** to join our multi\-facility, long\-term care organization as we expand and mature our **Institutional Special Needs Plan (ISNP)** and value\-based care strategy.



This is a high\-impact executive role for a physician leader who is energized by **population health, risk\-based models, and integrated care delivery**. The CMO will partner closely with executive leadership, operations, and clinical teams to drive initiatives that improve quality, outcomes, and performance across the enterprise.



This role offers a unique opportunity to help shape the future of long\-term care within a value\-based framework and to make a meaningful impact on the care of a large, medically complex population.


**Key Focus Areas**


* Medical Leadership for **ISNP and value\-based care initiatives.**
* Driving quality improvement, utilization management, and outcomes optimization.
* Aligning and leading **Medical Directors and physician partners.**
* Integrating medical strategy with **operational and clinical care models.**
* Advancing evidence\-based clinical initiatives across long\-term and post\-acute settings.


**Ideal Background**


* MD or DO required; board certification preferred.
* **Experience with ISNPs, Medicare Advantage, or value\-based care** strongly preferred.
* Prior leadership experience in long\-term care, post\-acute, managed care, or complex populations.
* Proven ability to lead at scale and collaborate across clinical and operational teams.


**Benefits and perks include:**


* Medical, vision \& dental insurance
* 401(k) retirement plan options
* Employer paid malpractice insurance
* Work\-Life Balance Time Off
* CME allowance
* Relocation assistance
* No call responsibilities
* Employee assistance program \& wellness support
* Retail, food \& entertainment discounts and so much more


* Full\-Time and Part\-Time Benefits may vary, terms and conditions apply


**About American Senior Communities**



Compassion, Accountability, Relationships and Excellence are the core values for American Senior Communities. These words not only form an acronym for C.A.R.E., but they are also our guiding principles and create the framework for all our relationships with customers, team members and community at large.



American Senior Communities has proudly served our customers since the year 2000, with a long history of excellent outcomes. Team members within each of our 100\+ American Senior Communities take great pride in our Hoosier hospitality roots, and it is ingrained in everything we do. As leaders in senior care, we are not just doing a job, but following a calling.

managers, digital team members, graphic designers and leadership team members – to  

coordinate client deliverables and due dates across multiple tasks and projects.
