title: eCommerce & Lifecycle Marketing Manager
description:
Description:  



**Who we are**


Founded in 2014 and based in Asheville, North Carolina, Poppy Handcrafted Popcorn Inc is a leading gourmet popcorn company in the U.S. We sell direct\-to\-consumer, wholesale and Grocery retail throughout the U.S. Poppy is the fastest\-growing small to mid\-sized CPG company in its category and boldly pursues ambitious goals that outperform the industry.


We believe in using only the best ingredients. We believe in spending way too much time thinking up flavor combinations and making way too many batches of popcorn to get those recipes just right. We believe in savory. And sweet. And we especially believe in savory and sweet. Simply, Poppy makes the best popcorn you will ever eat.


We are a people first organization and offer a professionally challenging and rewarding work environment. We are looking for innovative, creative, high energy, and talented professionals that have a passion for growing strong brands and products, thriving in an entrepreneurial environment, and building and leading fun, high\-performing teams.

**Location**


Asheville, NC – Hybrid Role. A balance of in\-person and virtual work. Hybrid employees work from our Asheville headquarters at least 3 days a week.

**Position Description**


The **E\-Commerce \& Lifecycle Marketing Manager** will drive the performance of Poppy’s digital storefronts (Shopify, Amazon, and others) while also owning the customer lifecycle strategy across email, SMS, and loyalty. This role will be responsible for managing the daily operations of the site, including lifecycle marketing, delivering and leading technical product roadmap, and merchandising enhancements to drive conversion, retention and revenue growth. We are seeking someone who is detail\-oriented and consumer journey obsessed.


This role blends strategic thinking with hands\-on execution across e\-commerce management, website optimization, customer segmentation, email flows, promotional planning, and analytics. You will work cross\-functionally with Marketing, Creative, Customer Service and Operations teams to deliver a seamless, high\-performing digital experience that reflects the Company’s brand values and business goals.

**Responsibilities**

**Ecommerce Strategy \& Website Management**

* Oversee and execute a holistic sales strategy across Shopify and Amazon.
* Manage and update Shopify\+, optimizing for conversion rates, AOV, and customer acquisition costs while also ensuring a premium, consistent brand experience across devices.
* Oversee content updates, seasonal merchandising, promotions and cross\-sell, and product launches within Shopify’s CMS and app ecosystem.
* Implement promotions, landing pages, and site navigation updates in collaboration with internal and external teams.

**Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO)**

* Lead A/B testing initiatives and implement data\-driven improvements to enhance the customer journey to improve funnel metrics, AOV and LTV.
* Identify and resolve UX friction points to boost site performance and conversions.
* Apply rigorous QA practices to ensure visual and functional consistency across platforms.
* Collaborate with external partners for paid media, SEO, SMS and tech stack management as needed.

**Lifecycle Marketing \& Retention**

* Own strategy and execution of email and SMS marketing via Klaviyo.
* Optimize automated flows (welcome, cart abandonment, post\-purchase, winback, etc.).
* Develop segmentation strategies to increase personalization and engagement.
* A/B test subject lines, creative, send times, and CTAs to improve performance.
* Manage customer retention initiatives, including loyalty programs, subscription models, and opportunities for surprise \& delight.
* Collaborate with creative to produce high\-performing campaign content and promotions.

**Technical \& Analytical Execution**

* Light touch HTML, CSS, and Liquid coding experience to make front\-end updates and resolve site issues quickly.
* Manage and oversee developers and vendors on new integrations, apps, and optimizations.
* Track key performance metrics, prepare site reports, and analyze conversion, cohort and basket trends to inform strategy.

**Customer Experience \& Copywriting**

* Obsess over the customer journey, striving to reduce friction and deliver


value at every touchpoint.

* Craft concise, persuasive, on\-brand copy for product descriptions, promotions, and landing pages in partnership with the Marketing / Creative team.
* Own site QA—ensuring content accuracy, pricing integrity, and a polished user experience.

**Key Metrics:**

* Revenue per session
* Conversion rate and funnel metrics
* Repeat Rate
* Average order value (AOV)
* Lifetime value (CLV), including Subscription revenue
* LTV
* Email performance metrics


Requirements:  



**Qualifications**

* 3–6 years of hands\-on experience managing Shopify / Shopify Plus sites, including Klaviyo and Recharge, with preference for CPG experience in food or natural products
* High attention to detail and strong organizational skills \- fully aware that every typo or misrepresentation can hurt our brand reputation and sales funnel
* Proficient in Shopify CMS, with working knowledge of Liquid, HTML, and CSS
* Demonstrated track record of driving CRO strategies and improving conversion metrics
* Strong understanding of customer journeys, segmentation and retention strategy
* Strong analytical and reporting skills—comfortable with Shopify Analytics, GA4, and Excel/Sheets
* Excellent copywriting and marketing judgment—able to produce short clean, brand\-aligned copy that converts, in conjunction with the creative team
* Understanding of SEO or UX best practices

**What We Offer**

* Competitive hourly pay or salary, depending on experience
* Employer Subsidized Health Insurance Plans
* Voluntary Supplemental Coverage Options
* PTO and paid holidays
* Fun, collaborative and values\-driven work environment
* Fabulous popcorn perks!

**Reports To**


Fractional CMO

**Collaborates With**


Marketing, Sales, Ops, Customer Service

**Direct Reports**


None

**Classification**


This position is full\-time, exempt

**Estimated Weekly Hours**


40\+ hrs, Monday \- Friday

**Physical Requirements**


Must be able to sit for extended periods, use standard office equipment and occasionally stand, walk or lift up to 20 lbs when handling product samples or light shipping materials.

title: Head of GTM (Outbound)
description:
We're hiring a **Head of GTM (Outbound)!**


### **Summary**


* **Flexible Title:** Can tailor to reflect your skills \& experience.
* **Flexible Time:** Can do full\-time, part\-time, side\-gig (off\-hours), or fractional (contract).
* **Flexible Commitment:** Can do short\-term, long\-term, or intermittent.


**Why so flexible?** We're a FUNDED startup racing to launch end of Q2 2026\. That gives us just 3 months to stack features while raising additional working capital. Feel free to jump in, help us ship, then bounce \>\> or stick around. A successful launch translates into lots of permanent jobs for those that want them. We're also interested in long term "side gig" relationships, if that's what you're into \- in our experience, a few expert hours often beat full\-time learning\-curve hours.


### **About Us**


We're a credible, **funded**, remote\-first startup led by a serial \[technical] founder, and backed by a 20\-person team. The product is live in private alpha.


Learn more about our founder, team, and comp structures at **list\-lab.org**.


### **About The Role**


This is the other half of our marketing leadership. Our Fractional CMO owns the narrative — brand, content, storytelling, category creation. **You own distribution.** Specifically: outbound acquisition through direct outreach, automation, and AI\-powered systems across email, LinkedIn, Facebook, DMs, groups, and communities.


No paid ads. No performance marketing. No CPMs. We meet people where they already hustle — social media, forums, DMs — and start conversations about their pain. The same systems serve both user acquisition and capital acquisition.


**This is a 0\-to\-1 role.** There is no existing playbook. There is no outbound motion to inherit and optimize. You're building it — from first experiment to scalable system. You'll work side by side with GTM engineers to stand up experiments fast, get data, kill what doesn't work, double down on what does, and turn winners into repeatable systems. Then you step back, work the numbers, and figure out how to scale.


You'll also manage a growing team of outbound specialists — email ops, LinkedIn automation, Facebook/social outreach, manual DM reps, GTM hackers, and pay\-per\-lead vendors. But you're not a delegator on day one. You're in the trenches building the machine before you run it.


This is a **technical, hands\-on outreach leadership role** — not a brand, content, or "marketing manager" role. If your instinct is "let's run some ads" or you need someone else to build the systems you manage — this isn't the right fit.


### **Compensation**


Up to **$400,000** max total compensation in Tier 1 cities; cash and equity components to be negotiated (amount reflects combined cash and equity components).


### **What Success Looks Like in 30 Days**


* You've designed and launched experiments across multiple outbound channels — email, LinkedIn, Facebook, social DMs — with your GTM engineers, and you have real data coming back
* You've identified which channels show signal and which are noise — and you've already killed at least one that isn't working
* You've stood up tracking and measurement so we can see what's happening across every experiment in one place
* You've started turning your first winning experiment into a repeatable system — documented, automated where possible, and ready to hand off or scale

You've demonstrated that you can move at startup speed: experiment data decision* next experiment, measured in days not weeks


### **What You'll Do**


* **Build outbound from zero** — there is no existing motion. You design the experiments, stand up the infrastructure, and create the playbook from scratch
* **Work hands\-on with GTM engineers** — you're in the tools alongside them: configuring sequences, wiring up automations, reviewing data, iterating on copy and targeting in real time
* **Run fast experiments** — design, launch, measure, and kill or scale outbound experiments across email, LinkedIn, Facebook, social DMs, and community channels. Cycle time is days, not sprints
* **Step back and work the numbers** — once experiments produce data, you analyze what's working, model CAC by channel, and decide where to pour gas and where to cut
* **Scale what works into systems** — turn winning experiments into repeatable, automated playbooks that can be handed off to specialists or run by AI
* **Manage the outbound team** — as experiments mature, you lead email ops, LinkedIn automation, Facebook/social outreach, manual DM reps, GTM hackers, and pay\-per\-lead vendors. You set targets, run accountability, and keep everyone moving fast
* **Push AI automation hard** — identify where AI agents, AI\-varied messaging, and automated flows can replace manual effort without sacrificing quality or stealth
* **Protect stealth** — we operate in stealth mode. Outreach must feel organic, never expose the product prematurely, and keep us off competitors' radar
* **Collaborate with the CMO** — align outbound messaging with brand narrative; ensure what you say in DMs matches what the website says
* **Report on performance with clarity** and take ownership of results — good or bad


### **What We're Looking For**


* **Technical and hands\-on** — you can configure outreach tools, wire up automations, build dashboards, and run experiments yourself. You're not someone who draws strategy decks and waits for others to execute
* **0\-to\-1 experience** — you've built an outbound motion from nothing before, not just inherited and optimized one. You know what it feels like when nothing is working yet and you have to find signal fast
* **Deep fluency with GTM tooling** — Clay, Phantom Buster, Apollo, Instantly, Dripify, HubSpot, Zapier/Make, GoHighLevel, or similar. You've built with these tools, not just managed people who use them
* **AI\-native** — you're already using AI to vary copy, personalize at scale, automate sequences, and compress cycle time. You have strong opinions about what works and what's hype
* **Data\-driven decision maker** — you build the dashboards, track CAC by channel, model unit economics, and make kill/scale decisions from the numbers. No vibes, no vanity metrics
* **Fast experimenter** — your default mode is: hypothesis experiment data decision. You measure in days, not quarters. You kill losers fast and double down on winners
* **Can lead a team** — you've managed outbound specialists, SDRs/BDRs, or GTM operators. You know how to set targets, run accountability, and get results from people across skill levels. But you're also comfortable being the best individual contributor on the team when needed
* **Systems thinker** — you see the whole outreach pipeline, not just individual channels. You know how to make email, LinkedIn, and social DMs work together instead of in silos
* **Startup speed** — you thrive in environments where there's no playbook, things change daily, and the answer to "what should we try next?" is "let's find out by tomorrow"
* **Stealth\-aware** — you understand why early\-stage companies need to be deliberate about what they expose and to whom


### **What You're NOT**


* Not a marketing manager who delegates everything and lives in slide decks
* Not a brand marketer, content strategist, or creative director (that's our CMO)
* Not a paid media buyer or performance marketer — we don't run ads
* Not someone who needs a finished playbook handed to them — you build the playbook
* Not someone who needs clean data and a stable operation before they can do their job — you create both from nothing

title: Now Hiring - Chief Marketing Officer (CMO) - Growth & Acquisition Architect
company: How To MANAGE A Small Law Firm
description:
**About How to Manage a Small Law Firm (HTM)**
----------------------------------------------


How to Manage a Small Law Firm (HTM) is dedicated to helping entrepreneurial attorneys run their firms like real businesses. Through coaching, strategy, and operational support, we help small law firm owners build sustainable and profitable practices.


Many lawyers are exceptional legal professionals but have never been taught how to lead, market, or scale a business. Our mission is to help law firm owners implement the systems, strategy, and leadership needed to grow their firms into highly successful businesses.


We specifically work with firms that are **scaling toward and through the $10M–$30M revenue stage**, where marketing systems, data\-driven decision making, and leadership discipline become critical.


We are seeking an experienced **Fractional Chief Marketing Officer (CMO)** who has helped businesses successfully **scale from $10M to $30M in revenue** and can guide our clients through that same growth journey.


**The Role**
============


As a **Fractional CMO**, you will work directly with law firm owners to diagnose marketing challenges, design direct\-response strategies, and ensure execution through coaching and accountability.


You will combine **strategic marketing leadership, analytical thinking, and business coaching** to help attorneys transform their marketing from guesswork into predictable growth systems capable of supporting firms scaling from **$10M to $30M in annual revenue**.


This is not a traditional in\-house CMO role. Instead, you will serve as a **strategic advisor across multiple law firms**, helping owners develop clear marketing strategies, evaluate vendors, and implement systems that drive measurable growth.


Most work will take place remotely via video meetings, where you will build trusted relationships with firm owners and influence outcomes through clear communication, insight, and leadership.


**Key Responsibilities**
========================


**Strategic Marketing Leadership**
----------------------------------


* Conduct marketing diagnostics with law firm owners to identify strengths, gaps, and growth opportunities.
* Design and guide **direct\-response marketing strategies** that drive measurable client acquisition.
* Help firms develop marketing systems including **funnels, calls\-to\-action, and list\-building strategies**.
* Evaluate existing marketing campaigns and realign strategies when ROI is not being achieved.

**Client Coaching \& Accountability**
-------------------------------------


* Coach law firm owners on marketing strategy, execution, and performance improvement.
* Provide constructive feedback when marketing efforts are not producing results.
* Balance encouragement with accountability to ensure agreed\-upon actions are implemented.
* Help clients overcome common entrepreneurial mindset challenges such as perfectionism or hesitation.

**Performance \& Metrics**
--------------------------


* Identify and prioritize key marketing KPIs across multiple campaigns.
* Use CRM systems, dashboards, and analytics to track marketing performance.
* Present marketing data in clear, actionable ways that drive better business decisions.
* Lead quarterly strategic reviews with clients and marketing vendors.

**Vendor \& Team Collaboration**
--------------------------------


* Work closely with internal HTM team members and external marketing vendors.
* Evaluate vendor performance and ensure alignment with client strategy.
* Facilitate productive discussions when vendors disagree on marketing direction.

**What Success Looks Like**
===========================


In your first **90 days**, you will:


* Build strong relationships with HTM clients and internal team members
* Conduct marketing diagnostics for assigned law firms
* Identify quick\-win improvements to underperforming campaigns
* Implement KPI tracking and reporting systems
* Lead strategic quarterly reviews focused on measurable results

Long term success means helping law firm owners turn marketing into a **predictable growth engine that supports firms scaling through the $10M–$30M revenue range.**


**Ideal Candidate Profile**
===========================


We are looking for someone who is both **strategic and coach\-oriented**.


You may be a strong fit if you:


* MUST HAVE 10\+ YEARS EXPERIENCE AS A CHIEF MARKETING OFFICER
* Have served as a **CMO, marketing strategist, or senior marketing consultant**
* Have experience working with **growing service\-based businesses**
* Enjoy coaching entrepreneurs and business owners
* Are comfortable providing honest, constructive feedback
* Can quickly diagnose marketing challenges and identify root causes
* Thrive in a **remote, client\-facing leadership role**

**Required Experience**
=======================


* **Proven experience helping businesses scale from $10M to $30M in revenue**
* Significant experience designing and leading **direct\-response marketing campaigns**
* Demonstrated track record of driving **measurable ROI**
* Experience working with **small to mid\-sized service businesses**
* Strong understanding of **funnels, lead generation, conversion optimization, and list\-building**
* Experience evaluating marketing vendors and campaign performance
* Ability to translate marketing data into **clear business decisions**

**This Role Is Not for You If**
===============================


* You prefer a slow\-paced corporate environment
* You dislike accountability or performance metrics
* You prefer task\-based marketing execution rather than strategic leadership
* You struggle managing multiple clients simultaneously
* You have never supported or led a **small\-to\-mid\-size service Business**

**Compensation**
================


**Base Salary:** $110,000  

**Performance Pay:** Up to $182,000 total annual compensation depending on client roster and performance.


Your earning potential scales with the **impact and results you deliver — not simply the hours you work.**


If you are an execution\-focused leader who thrives on accountability and enjoys helping business owners follow through on what matters most, this role will challenge and reward you. **Apply only if you are ready to lead from the front and deliver real results.**


Please note this job description is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities that are required of the employee for this job. Duties, responsibilities and activities may change at any time with or without notice.


At this time, we are ***only*** able to hire new employees in the following states, **AZ, CO, FL, GA, IL, MD, MI, NC, NJ, NY, PA, TX, VA, WA.**


*HTM provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws.*

title: Marketing Manager
description:
**Marketing Manager (In\-Office)**

  

Polaris Estate Planning Elder Law


St. Charles, MO

  

Help Lead Marketing for a Growing Professional Firm

  

Polaris Estate Planning \& Elder Law is looking for a **Marketing Manager** to help drive the next phase of growth for our firm.

  

We are an established and expanding estate planning and elder law firm serving families throughout the St. Louis and St. Charles region. Our mission is simple:

**Help families make wise legal decisions before a crisis forces them to.**

  

Our marketing foundation is already in place. We currently work with:

* A **fractional Chief Marketing Officer**
* An **in\-house Marketing Coordinator**
* Established **SEO and website infrastructure**
* Educational **seminars and workshops**
* **Video and content marketing initiatives**
* A steady flow of **client inquiries**

  

What we need now is a **strong marketing operator and team leader** who can bring structure, organization, and execution power to our marketing systems.

  

If you enjoy **running campaigns, improving marketing systems, and seeing measurable results from your work**, this role offers a unique opportunity to make a real impact inside a growing professional firm.

  

Compensation

* Base Salary: **$60,000 – $70,000** (based on experience)
* Performance Bonuses available.
* Total Compensation Potential: **$70,000 – $85,000**
* Bonuses are tied to measurable marketing execution, campaign performance, and marketing system improvements.
* Strong performers will have **clear advancement opportunities**, including the potential to grow into a **Senior Marketing Manager role** as the firm expands.

**Compensation:**
$60,000 \- $70,000

**Responsibilities:**
This role sits at the center of the firm’s marketing operations.


You will work directly with leadership and our Chief Marketing Officer to **execute campaigns, manage marketing systems, and oversee the firm’s marketing coordinator.**

 **Your responsibilities will include:**


Marketing Campaign Execution

* Execute marketing initiatives developed with leadership and the CMO
* Coordinate campaigns across digital, email, seminars, and referral channels
* Manage marketing timelines and ensure projects stay on schedule


Digital Marketing Coordination

* Maintain website accuracy and brand consistency
* Support SEO\-driven content marketing initiatives
* Assist with email marketing campaigns and automation
* Coordinate digital campaign assets


Brand \& Marketing Materials

* Maintain consistency of the Polaris brand across all materials
* Oversee creation of seminar materials, presentations, and handouts
* Ensure professional and consistent marketing assets


Video \& Educational Marketing

* Help coordinate educational video marketing initiatives
* Maintain consistency in messaging and brand presentation
* Organize video marketing campaigns


Marketing Systems \& CRM Infrastructure

* Maintain marketing workflows and automation systems
* Support lead tracking and reporting
* Ensure marketing data and reporting systems remain organized


Reporting \& Metrics

* Monitor marketing campaign performance
* Maintain marketing dashboards and reporting
* Provide performance insights to leadership


Team Leadership

* Supervise and guide the **In\-House Marketing Coordinator**
* **Ensure marketing initiatives are executed consistently and professionally**
* **Coordinate marketing efforts across internal teams**

**Qualifications:**

We are looking for someone who is **organized, proactive, and execution\-focused.**


Ideal candidates will have:

* 2–5 years of marketing experience (agency or in\-house)
* Experience coordinating and executing marketing campaigns
* Strong organizational and project management skills
* Experience creating or coordinating professional marketing materials
* Familiarity with digital marketing tools and email marketing platforms
* Ability to manage multiple marketing initiatives simultaneously


Helpful but not required:

* Experience with **CRM platforms** such as Lawmatics
* Basic **video editing or video coordination** experience
* Experience working within **established brand guidelines**
* Marketing experience in **professional services** (law, finance, healthcare)

**About Company**  

Polaris Estate Planning \& Elder Law is a mission\-driven law firm dedicated to helping families protect what they’ve built and prepare for life’s most important transitions with clarity and confidence. Based in Missouri and serving clients throughout the St. Charles and St. Louis areas, our firm focuses exclusively on estate planning, elder law, asset protection, probate, and long\-term care planning.

**We are not a volume\-based law firm.**

**We are a relationship\-based planning firm.**


Our team works with individuals and families who want more than just documents — they want a thoughtful plan that protects their loved ones, avoids unnecessary court involvement, and prepares for the realities of aging, long\-term care, and unexpected life events.

**Our mission is simple:**


To help families make wise legal and life decisions before a crisis forces them to. We don’t just prepare documents — we help families protect their future, their legacy, and the people they love most.

title: RN - Cath Lab
description:
**Employment Type:**  

Full time
  
  

**Shift:**  

Day Shift
  
  

**Description:**  

**Department:** Heart Catheterization Lab
  
**Location:** Trinity Health Ann Arbor
  
About the Department
  
Heart Catheterization and Electrophysiology (EP) Lab
  
Our state\-of\-the\-art Heart Catheterization (Cath) and Electrophysiology (EP) Lab is dedicated to providing comprehensive cardiovascular care. We operate five advanced labs, including two dedicated EP labs, two Cath labs, and one versatile swing lab that can accommodate both types of procedures.
  
**Hybrid OR Capabilities:** Our facilities include a cutting\-edge Hybrid Operating Room (OR) that allows for seamless integration of both Cath and EP procedures, ensuring the highest level of patient care and procedural efficiency. Our hybrid cases focus on Transcatheter Aortic Valve Replacement (TAVR), Watchman device implantation, Mitral Valve Repair (MVR), and device explants.
  
  

**Case Volume:**  

* Catheterization (Cath) Cases: We handle around 10\-14 Cath cases each day, focusing on diagnostic and interventional procedures such as coronary angiography, percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI), stent placement, intravascular ultrasound (IVUS), fractional flow reserve (FFR) measurements, Chronic Total Occlusion (CTO) procedures, and treatment of STEMI (ST\-Elevation Myocardial Infarction).


Our team of highly skilled cardiologists, electrophysiologists, nurses, and technologists work collaboratively to deliver exceptional care, utilizing the latest technology and techniques to achieve the best possible outcomes for our patients.
  
  

**Shifts Available:** Varied shifts
  
  

REQUIRED EDUCATION AND LICENSURE
  
  

* Graduation from accredited nursing program.
* All new hires must have a BSN degree or must agree in writing to obtain their BSN within five (5\) years from date of hire or transfer to an RN role.
* Current Michigan Nursing License
* BLS


REQUIRED SKILLS AND ABILITIES
  
Knowledge of nursing theory, practice and age specific needs Interpersonal skills necessary to initiate and maintain collegial relationships with coworkers and therapeutic relationships with patients and families. Critical thinking skills necessary to perform principal duties and responsibilities of job description. Ability to concentrate and pay close attention to detail when planning and performing professional nursing care.
  
  

**Total Rewards and Benefits:**  

* Competitive compensation,DAILYPAY
* Benefits effective Day One! No waiting periods.
* Full benefits package including Medical, Dental, Vision, PTO, Life Insurance, Short and Long\-term Disability
* Retirement savings plan with employer match and contributions
* Opportunity for growth and advancement throughout SJMHS and Trinity Health
* Tuition Reimbursement


**What you will do:**  

* View patients holistically; considers physical, psychosocial, emotional, age specific, cultural and spiritual factors. Demonstrate appropriate assessment skills for identified patient populations.
* Demonstrate critical thinking skills by gathering and analyzing patient information, then responding to a patient situation.
* Develop, implement, evaluate and revise an individualized plan of care using evidenced based interventions and standards of care. Identify needs and begins to plan for care across the continuum considering patient and family preferences.
* Identify patient and family education needs. Develop and implement teaching plans based on evidence\-based practice, seeking out additional resources when necessary. Utilize teach back methodology to assure patient and family is learning effectively. Identify necessary resources to assure readiness for discharge and begins to develop plan in collaboration with other disciplines.
* Create an environment that promotes a positive patient experience.
* Communicate both verbal and written information (including documentation in the EMR) that is accurate, timely, concise and thorough. Effectively communicate patient status, priority goals and interventions to colleagues and other disciplines during consultation, rounds and handoffs.
* Maintains good rapport and cooperative relationships. Approaches conflict in a constructive manner. Helps to identify problems, offer solutions, and participate in their resolution.


Our Commitment
  
  

Rooted in our Mission and Core Values, we honor the dignity of every person and recognize the unique perspectives, experiences, and talents each colleague brings. By finding common ground and embracing our differences, we grow stronger together and deliver more compassionate, person\-centered care. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status, or any other status protected by federal, state, or local law.

title: Fractional Chief AI Officer - AI Transformation, Engineering Leadership
description:
Project Brief
  
OneSeven Tech is expanding its strategic AI capabilities to meet an upcoming pipeline of high\-impact digital transformation projects. We are looking for an "Operator" who can bridge the gap between business vision and technical execution, ensuring AI implementation drives measurable growth rather than just experimentation. This role acts as a high\-level advisor to help traditional businesses and startups transition into AI\-first organizations.
  
Must\-Haves
  
* AI Transformation \& Implementation: Proven track record of leading AI strategy from discovery to deployment.
* 10\+ years of Engineering Leadership: Extensive experience as a CTO, VP of Engineering, or Engineering Lead in digital product environments.
* Fractional Consulting Background: Must be currently operating in a fractional, interim, or advisory capacity with multiple successful engagements.
* Digital Transformation Expertise: History of leading complex migrations from legacy or standard digital systems to AI\-integrated workflows.
* Outcome\-Oriented Mindset: Ability to demonstrate clear ROI, such as significant time reductions or increased operational capacity through technology.


Nice to Have
  
* AI Certification: Formal certification as a Chief AI Officer or equivalent AI Executive training.
* B2B Agency Experience: Experience working within or for digital product studios or B2B professional services firms.
* Cybersecurity/Governance Knowledge: Experience in information security governance, AI risk management, and bias controls.
* High\-Pressure Leadership: Experience in decision\-making under pressure and a "constant pursuit of excellence" (Special Forces or similar background is a plus).


What You Will Do
  
* Architect AI Strategies: Build custom AI adoption frameworks that enhance human expertise and scale output.
* Drive Implementation: Partner with product and engineering teams to operationalize AI across targeting, content, and revenue analytics.
* Manage AI Governance: Own the strategy for AI safety, culture, and ethical execution to ensure long\-term investment stability.
* C\-Suite Advisory: Act as a "Sidecar" to leadership teams, translating technical breakthroughs into actionable business plays.
* Audit \& Optimization: Conduct deep\-dive assessments to identify productivity gains and eliminate operational redundancies.


Why This Could Be Your Next Big Move
  
* High Influence \& Impact: Work directly with leadership to shape the AI future of a premier digital product agency.
* Diverse Project Pipeline: Lead AI strategy for a variety of startups and enterprise partners that have raised over $100M in VC.
* Technical Innovation: Solve complex problems by architecting agent\-driven workflows and predictive models from scratch.
* Elite Professional Network: Join a fast\-moving team that values "culture\-first" transformation and elegant, high\-performance engineering.


Benefits \& Compensation
  
* $100 \- $150 USD/hour: Competitive hourly rate based on experience.
* Fully Remote: Work from anywhere in the United States.
* Long\-term contract: Starting with a 6\-month engagement with the intention of continuity based on pipeline.
* Referral Program: Earn a bonus for referring top\-tier talent to our network.


To Apply
  
  

**Please send your resume in English. Include the following:**  

* LinkedIn Profile URL (required).
* GitHub Repository or Portfolio (required for engineering/product focus).
* Relevant Certifications (e.g., CAIO, AWS, Azure).
* Cover Letter: Tell us about a time you turned a manual business process into a 10x output via technology.


About OneSeven Tech
  
Founded by James Sullivan, OneSeven Tech is a premier digital product agency serving startups and enterprises. Our clients have collectively raised over $100M in VC, and our enterprise partners include NASDAQ\-listed companies. We move fast with 1\-week sprints and focus on elegant solutions to complex problems.

title: Principal Engineer, Platform & Infrastructure
description:
**About** **Silo**

 ***Help us rebuild the oldest system in the world \- the agricultural supply chain***

  

Silo builds technologies which bring efficiency and transparency to the agricultural supply chain. Our modern ERP dramatically increases profits, improves product freshness, and reduces food waste for wholesalers, distributors, shippers, and importers.

  

We are a highly motivated team of engineers, designers, business and agricultural specialists taking on the challenge of transforming a centuries old industry. We are looking for self\-motivated team members who crave a challenge and feel energized to work cross functionally to realize Silo’s enormous potential.

 **Why Silo**

  

We will work hard to make sure your decision to join us is worthwhile and rewarding. You’ll learn how a start\-up operates and learn skills that will help you at any point in your career. You will be empowered to take initiative and get support to execute an idea from beginning to the end.

 **ENGINEERING · REMOTE · $160,000 – $210,000**

**Lead Engineer**

 *Silo Technologies · Remote (United States) · Full\-Time*

**The Role**


This is a Lead Engineer role — a step below CTO, but carrying real weight. You will be the most senior full\-time technical individual contributor and de facto owner of Silo’s production platform. You’ll work directly with our Product Head, under the guidance of our fractional CTO during transition, and be responsible for the stability, performance, and architecture of our core systems.

  

You’re not here to manage a team of ten. You’re here to get things working, set standards, mentor others, and own the technical outcome.

 **What You’ll Do**

**Platform Stability \& Incident Response**

* Take primary ownership of production system reliability — including on\-call response, root cause analysis, and remediation
* Lead coordination with our external engineering partner on technical escalations, incident resolution, and performance improvements
* Drive improvements to our incident management processes, from detection to resolution to post\-mortems

 **Architecture \& Performance**

* Identify and resolve performance bottlenecks — especially in SQL query design, Postgres database architecture, and Go service performance
* Define and implement technical standards for scalability, observability, and resilience across the platform
* Lead the technical integration of PetTiger’s labor management functionality into the Silo ERP

 **Technical Leadership**

* Work closely with our fractional CTO and CEO to define the long\-term architecture vision and engineering operational model
* Collaborate with Product to translate roadmap priorities into solid technical execution
* Mentor and level\-up the broader engineering team; establish a culture of ownership and craftsmanship, while using the most recent advancements in AI.
* Be the internal technical voice in decisions about tooling, vendor relationships, and build vs. buy

 **Hiring \& Team Building**

* Help define and grow the in\-house engineering organization over time
* Eventually become the engineering leader Silo needs for its next phase of growth

 **What We’re Looking For**

**Required**

* 8\+ years of software engineering experience, with at least 2–3 years in a senior, lead, or staff engineering capacity
* Track record of improving reliability in complex engineering environments, including defining SLAs with external teams, building escalation paths, and driving down incident frequency through systemic process improvement rather than heroics
* Deep expertise in one or more systems\-level backend languages (Go, Rust, C\+\+, Java, or similar) with strong working knowledge of at least one, and genuine willingness to work in Go as our primary backend language
* Expert\-level knowledge of **Postgres**, query optimization, indexing strategy, schema design, and performance tuning under load
* Proven experience partnering with and managing offshore or external engineering teams — including setting expectations, defining deliverables, running structured communication cadences, and holding vendors accountable to quality and uptime standards without micromanaging execution
* Strong communication skills — you need to translate complex technical issues for executive stakeholders and customers in a high\-stakes environment
* Comfortable operating in a startup environment where priorities shift and production issues demand immediate attention — you bring calm and structure to high\-pressure situations

 **Strongly Preferred**

* Experience with AWS, Kubernetes, and cloud\-native infrastructure
* Background in ERP, agtech, supply chain, or fintech domains
* Familiarity with AI\-assisted development workflows and modern developer tooling (we believe in embracing tools like Claude to accelerate engineering velocity)
* Experience defining technical processes: incident management, deployment pipelines, on\-call rotations
* Prior work at a PE\-backed or high\-growth company navigating scaling challenges

**Equal Opportunity Employer**


Silo Technologies is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable federal, state, or local law. We are committed to building a diverse and inclusive team and encourage applicants of all backgrounds to apply.

 *Silo Technologies is committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities. If you require an accommodation to participate in the application or interview process, please contact our People Operations team.*

 *Silo Technologies · usesilo.com · Fresno, CA*

title: Revenue Cycle Success Manager
description:
**About Joyful Health**
-----------------------

**Building the financial operating system for healthcare, and bringing the** ***joy*** **back to healthcare by fixing the financial chaos behind it.**


The healthcare payment system is a complex and inefficient maze \- healthcare practices leave $125 billion in revenue uncollected each year, lost in the chaos of fragmented financial data, manual workflows, and opaque payer systems. This financial uncertainty leaves practices struggling to stay afloat, while valuable revenue slips through the cracks.


Joyful Health is building the AI\-powered financial operating system for healthcare practices. Our mission is to bring the joy back to running a private practice by simplifying financial operations so providers can focus on patient care. We spent 10 months working as fractional CFOs for a dozen practices, doing this work side by side with providers as we developed our product.


We just closed a funding round led by world\-class investors and angels including the founders of MongoDB \& KAYAK.


We have an enormous opportunity in front of us. The broken healthcare payment ecosystem impacts practices of all sizes, and the opportunity to make a real difference is massive.


If you’re passionate about empowering medical practices and excited to tackle one of the most important (\& challenging!) problems in healthcare, we’d love to meet you.

**The Role**
------------

### **Customer success at Joyful Health isn’t a support function. It’s the strategic layer that ensures practices don’t just receive billing services — they experience improved outcomes.**

**This is a remote role with a preference for candidates in the NYC area, who would be open to a hybrid working model.**

The **Revenue Cycle Success Manager is one of the primary differentiators in the Joyful customer experience.** Our customers choose Joyful not only for our technology and billing infrastructure, but for the expertise and partnership they receive through their RSM. This role combines deep revenue cycle knowledge with strategic customer leadership, helping practices navigate the complexity of healthcare reimbursement while translating Joyful’s capabilities into measurable financial improvement.


The RCM Success Manager is the primary relationship owner for Joyful Health customers. You will own the executive relationship, the strategic narrative, QBRs, and escalation management end\-to\-end.


This role sits at the intersection of revenue cycle expertise, customer strategy, and product signal. RCM Success Managers at Joyful Health need to hear what their customers are really asking for, translate those needs to relevant cross\-functional team members, and help prioritize changes where necessary. This requires commercial instinct, analytical rigor, and product\-level thinking across the team. You’ll partner closely with our RCM Center of Excellence (CoE), pulling deep analyses and benchmarking insights, then translating that data into clear, actionable recommendations for practice leadership.


This is a role for someone who wants to operate like a mini product manager — someone who is product\-oriented and customer\-obsessed. The RSM role is accountable for Joyful customer outcomes, not just activity.

### **What you'll do…**

### **Own the Customer Relationship**

* Serve as the primary point of contact for your portfolio of practices
* Build trusted relationships with operational leaders and executives
* Ensure every practice feels like they have a dedicated strategic partner

### **Drive the Strategic Narrative**

* Translate performance data into a clear story
* Connect RCM metrics to business impact
* Define what the practice should prioritize next
* Move conversations from “what happened” to “what we do about it”

### **Lead QBRs \& Executive Engagement**

* Run structured quarterly business reviews
* Surface renewal risk and growth opportunities early
* Create alignment on goals and measurable outcomes

### **Own Escalations End\-to\-End**

* Coordinate across Operations, the CoE, Product, and Leadership
* Ensure issues are resolved with clarity and follow\-through
* Maintain control of communication during high\-pressure situations

### **Translate Customer Signal Into Internal Insight**

* Identify patterns across accounts
* Bring structured feedback to the Product teams
* Help prioritize improvements that strengthen our RCM model

**How You’ll Be Measured**
--------------------------

* Net Revenue Retention (NRR) across your portfolio
* Practice performance outcomes
* Renewal stability and risk mitigation
* Expansion identification
* Quality of executive engagement

**What Success Looks Like**
---------------------------

### **In 30 Days**

* Deep understanding of your accounts — performance trends, risks, and history
* Strong working relationships established internally and externally

### **In 90 Days**

* Leading QBRs independently
* Proactively surfacing renewal risks and expansion signals
* Escalations handled with structure and confidence

### **In 12 Months**

* Your portfolio demonstrates strong retention and clear expansion opportunities
* Practices consistently describe you as a strategic partner
* Your insights materially improve how Joyful operates

**We’d Love to Hear From You If…**
----------------------------------


You have:

* 5–8\+ years of experience in healthcare revenue cycle management, healthcare technology, or a related domain
* Experience working and/or managing denials and A/R in complex specialties such as ABA, IOP, Wound Care, etc.
* Direct experience working with medical practices, provider groups, or health systems in a vendor, customer success or consulting role.
* Strong working knowledge of key RCM metrics (AR aging, denial rates, collection rate, payer mix, cash acceleration, etc.)
* Experience owning or materially influencing retention, renewal, or expansion outcomes
* Experience leading executive\-level conversations (COO, CFO, practice owner)
* Experience running structured business reviews (QBRs or equivalent)


You are:

* Comfortable translating complex performance data into a clear business narrative
* Highly organized and able to manage 5–6 accounts end\-to\-end without dropping details
* Calm under pressure and capable of owning escalations through resolution
* Systems\-oriented — you recognize patterns across accounts, not just isolated issues
* Commercially minded — you understand that retention and expansion are outcomes, not accidents
* Strong written and verbal communicator with executive presence


Please apply even if you don’t meet 100% of the criteria. We care about operators who think like owners and want to grow alongside us.

**Our Value Prop to You**
-------------------------


At Joyful Health, we’re on a mission to fix one of the most broken systems in healthcare. Joining us means having the opportunity to make a direct, meaningful impact on the lives of clinicians and the financial health of their practices. Here’s what you can expect from working with us:

**Meaningful Impact** You’ll be tackling one of the most complex challenges in healthcare—helping practices thrive in a chaotic financial landscape. Your work will directly contribute to solving one of the most hair on fire problems for providers, ensuring that they can focus on what they do best: caring for patients.

**Autonomy \& Ownership** As an early team member, you’ll have significant autonomy and the chance to shape our company from the ground up. You’ll have ownership over your work and the freedom to challenge the status quo in an environment that values initiative and creativity.

**Growth \& Learning** We’re a small, ambitious team with big goals, which means you’ll be constantly learning and growing. You’ll take on new challenges, expand your skill set, and have direct access to experienced founders and advisors who are invested in your development. We also offer stipends for professional development courses \& books (that we often will partake in together!) so we can all continuously grow.

**Competitive Compensation \& Equity** We believe that our team is our most valuable asset. We offer competitive compensation packages, strong equity, and the opportunity to share in the success of Joyful Health. We want you to feel supported, both financially and professionally, as we grow together.

**Be Your Best Self** We provide the benefits you need to stay happy and healthy, including comprehensive healthcare benefits, unlimited PTO (with a minimum of 10 days off a year), and flexibility so you can take care of yourself. We also organize regular team off\-sites and events to ensure that even as we tackle big problems we enjoy the journey.

title: Fractional HR Generalist
description:
Position Overview:
**Job Title****: Fractional Human Resource Generalist**  

**Location:** Coconut Creek, FL 33066  

**Schedule:** Onsite, 28 hours per week  

**Position Type:** Part\-time

  

**Why join Junior Achievement of South Florida?**
Junior Achievement of South Florida (JA) empowers our youth with the knowledge, ability, and confidence to navigate their futures, drive our economy and lead our community. We are looking for individuals who are passionate not only about our mission, but also about being part of the innovative and energetic culture at JA.  

JA provides real\-world training in financial literacy including budgeting, spending, investing and the use of credit; offers cutting\-edge skill\-building opportunities that enable young people to explore meaningful, productive careers; teaches students how to start businesses; and introduces entrepreneurial values that strengthen workplaces. Last year, with the help of over 7,100 trained corporate and community volunteers, JA delivered over 20 various programs to almost 50,000 students in classrooms throughout Broward and south Palm Beach counties and at JA World Huizenga Center at the Lillian S. Wells Pavilion, a first\-class facility housing JA BizTown and JA Finance Park.  

For more information about Junior Achievement of South Florida, visit www.JASouthFlorida.org. Follow JA on social media @jasouthflorida. **What We Offer:**
✅ **Employer Paid Medical and Vision**


✅ **Ample Time Off**


✅ **Retirement Plan Matching**


✅ **Work Environment** – Innovative and energetic culture!  

✅ **Growth Opportunities** – Professional development assistance

 **Role Snapshot:**
The core function of your role as **Fractional Human Resources Generalist** is to focus on administrative and operational HR tasks while supporting staff development, compliance, and organizational culture. The HR Generalist works closely with the COO and external HR partner to ensure all aspects of Human Resources are managed.  

This is an ideal role for someone who thrives in a dynamic, mission\-driven environment, enjoys process management, and is skilled at supporting staff at all levels. **Your key responsibilities will include:** **HR Administration \& Operations**

* Administer HR processes for full\-time, part\-time, seasonal staff, and interns, for the full employee life cycle including recruiting, onboarding, training, offboarding, employee records, and benefits administration.
* Partner with the Finance Director on payroll\-related matters; ensure accurate execution and alignment with HR records.
* Serve as the primary point of contact for staff regarding HR policies, procedures, and general inquiries.
* Maintain accurate employee records and HR databases, ensuring compliance with local, state, and federal regulations.

 **Professional Development \& Engagement**

* Oversee professional development programs for staff, including workshops, training sessions, and performance support initiatives.
* Nurture and encourage a positive, value\-driven work environment that promotes employee engagement and collaboration.

 **Compliance \& Risk Management**

* Administer and execute routine tasks in delicate circumstances, including providing reasonable accommodation, investigating allegations of wrongdoing, and assisting with terminations.
* Maintain compliance with federal, state, and local employment laws and regulations, as well as recommended HR best practices, review policies and practices regularly.
* Collaborate with the Community Outreach Manager to ensure volunteer practices comply with applicable laws.
* Maintain knowledge of trends, best practices, regulatory changes, and new technologies in HR, talent management, and employment law.

 **Vendor \& Partner Management**

* Oversee partnerships with external HR service providers, delegating tasks and leading projects to ensure timely and accurate execution of HR tasks.
* Act as liaison between the organization and HR vendors to resolve issues and implement improvements.

 **Other Responsibilities**

* Support recruiting efforts for staff and seasonal interns, including assisting with onboarding interns, ensuring orientation and compliance requirements are met.
* Perform other duties as assigned.

* **The craft and expertise you’ll bring:**
* Comprehensive knowledge of federal, state, and local employment laws, HR best practices, and regulatory compliance requirements.
* Strong administrative and organizational skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines.
* Excellent interpersonal, communication, and conflict resolution skills; able to build trust and collaborate effectively across all levels of the organization.
* Ability to lead professional development initiatives and foster a positive, value\-driven workplace culture.
* Skilled in HR systems, databases, and technology tools; comfortable adopting innovative solutions, including AI tools, to streamline HR processes.
* Analytical and detail\-oriented, with the ability to identify trends, risks, and opportunities for process improvements.
* Ability to work independently, exercise sound judgment, and provide guidance on complex or sensitive HR matters.
* Comfort using HR technology platforms; ability to adopt innovative tools to enhance efficiency.

 **Education \& Experience:**

* Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or related field preferred.
* Minimum 3–5 years of HR experience, preferably in a generalist role supporting full\-time, part\-time, and temporary employees.
* Experience in employee relations, performance management, and handling sensitive HR issues with discretion and professionalism.
* Experience overseeing vendors or external partners is a plus.

 ***Along with the above, we would also expect you to:***

* Incorporate all of JA's 10 core values:
	+ **Commit to Growth:** Embrace continuous learning and development to achieve personal and professional advancement.
	+ **Deliver the Wow:** Consistently exceed expectations and create remarkable experiences for customers, clients, and colleagues.
	+ **Be bold, innovative, and creative:** Foster a culture of daring exploration, original thinking, and imaginative problem\-solving.
	+ **Do it with passion:** Approach tasks and challenges with enthusiasm, dedication, and a genuine love for what you do.
	+ **Own your actions:** Take responsibility for your decisions and outcomes and hold yourself accountable to high standards of integrity.
	+ **Build awesome relationships:** Cultivate strong, supportive connections with colleagues, clients, and stakeholders based on trust, respect, and collaboration.
	+ **Bring fun \& energy:** Infuse positivity, enjoyment, and vitality into the workplace environment and interactions.
	+ **Celebrate uniqueness:** Value diversity in perspectives, backgrounds, and contributions, fostering an inclusive and supportive atmosphere.
	+ **Drive and embrace change:** Proactively seek opportunities for innovation and improvement, adapting positively to evolving circumstances and challenges.
	+ **Be true, be you:** Embrace authenticity, integrity, and honesty in all interactions, staying true to your values and identity.

 **Important Information:**
 Junior Achievement of South Florida participates in the federal government’s E\-Verify program, which confirms employment authorization of all newly hired employees and most existing employees through an electronic database maintained by the Social Security Administration and Department of Homeland Security. For all new hires, the E\-Verify process is completed in conjunction with the Form I\-9 Employment Eligibility Verification on or before the first day of work. E\-Verify is not used as a tool to pre\-screen candidates. For up\-to\-date information on E\-Verify, go to www.uscis.gov and click on 'E\-Verify' located near the bottom of the page. **An Equal Opportunity Employer**
We do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, sex, age, disability, genetic information, or any other status protected by law or regulation. It is our intention that all qualified applicants are given equal opportunity and that selection decisions be based on job\-related factors. *JA provides real\-world training in financial literacy, including budgeting, spending, investing, and the use of credit; offers cutting\-edge skill\-building opportunities that enable young people to explore meaningful, productive careers; teaches students how to start businesses; and introduces entrepreneurial values that strengthen workplaces. Last year, with the help of over 5,000 trained corporate and community volunteers, JA delivered various programs to almost 70,000 students in classrooms throughout Broward and South Palm Beach counties and at JA World Huizenga Center at the Lillian S. Wells Pavilion, a first\-class facility housing JA BizTown and JA Finance Park. For more information about Junior Achievement of South Florida, visit www.JASouthFlorida.org. Follow JA on social media @jasouthflorida.* **Apply Today!**
